concepts.title: Understanding the process of Substitution
concepts.conceptBody:
Understanding the process of Substitution 

Let it be required to find the integral $\int ~f\left( x \right)dx$ we cannot directly select the antiderivative of $f\left( x \right)$ but we know that it exists. Let us change the variable in the expression under the integral sign, putting $\text{x}=\phi \left( \text{t} \right)$ … (1)

where $\phi \left( \text{t} \right)$ is a continuous function (with continuous derivative) having an inverse function.

The $dx={\phi }'\left( t \right)dt$, we shall prove that in this case we have the following equation :

$\int ~f\left( x \right)dx=\int ~f\left[ \phi \left( t \right) \right]{\phi }'\left( t \right)dt$ … (2)

The following are some instances of integration by substitution.

concepts.title: Substitution done in cases where the integrand is in the form of ([f(x)]^n).f'(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Method of Substitution 

Type $1:\int \frac{{f}'\left( x \right)}{f\left( x \right)}dx$ 

This is a very common type of substitution.
put $\text{f}\left( \text{x} \right)=\text{t}$, then
$\Rightarrow \text{{f}'}\left( \text{x} \right)\text{dx}=\text{dt}$, so the integral becomes
$\int \frac{{f}'\left( x \right)}{f\left( x \right)}dx=\int \frac{dt}{t}=\text{log}\left| t \right|+c$
$\therefore \int \frac{{f}'\left( x \right)}{f\left( x \right)}dx=\text{log}\left| f\left( x \right) \right|+c$
You can also remember this by the following method
$\int \frac{\text{ derivative }\text{ of }\text{ denominator }}{\text{ denominator }}=\text{log}\mid \text{ denominator }\mid +c$
Let us prove sum standard results using this property.

Illustration 13 

Find : 
(a) $\int \text{tan}xdx$ 
(b) $\int \text{cot}xdx$ 
(c) $\int \text{sec}xdx$ 
(d) $\int \text{cosec}xdx$ 

Solution: 

Though we have already learnt the formulas for the above integrals, let us now prove them

(a) $\int \text{tan}xdx=\int \frac{\text{tan}x\text{sec}x}{\text{sec}x}dx$

and we know $\frac{d}{dx}\left( \text{sec}x \right)=\text{tan}x\text{sec}x$

hence $\frac{{f}'\left( x \right)}{f\left( x \right)}$ form

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\int \text{tan}x=\text{log}\left| \text{sec}x \right|+c$

(b) $\int \text{cot}xdx=\int \frac{\text{cos}x}{\text{sin}x}dx$

$\frac{d}{dx}\left( \text{sin}x \right)=\text{cos}x$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\int \text{cotx}d\text{x}=\text{log}\left| \text{sinx} \right|\text{dx}$

(c) $\int \text{sec}xdx=\int \frac{\text{sec}x\left( \text{sec}x+\text{tan}x \right)}{\text{sec}x+\text{tan}x}dx$

$=\int ~\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x+\text{sec}x+\text{tan}x}{\text{tan}x+\text{sec}x}dx$

$\frac{d}{dx}\left( \text{tan}x+\text{sec}x \right)=\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x+\text{sec}x\text{tan}x$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\int \text{secx}d\text{x}=\text{log}\left| \text{secx}+\text{tanx} \right|+c$

(d) $\int \text{cosec}xdx=\int \frac{\text{cosec}x\left( \text{cosec}x-\text{cot}x \right)}{\left( \text{cosec}x-\text{cot}x \right)}dx$

$~=\int ~\frac{\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x-\text{cosec}x\text{cot}x}{\text{cosec}x-\text{cot}x}dx$

$\frac{d}{dx}\left( \text{cosec}x-\text{cot}x \right)=-\text{cosec}x\text{cot}x+\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ $~\int ~\text{cosec}xdx=\text{log}\left| \text{cosec}x-\text{cot}x \right|+c$

Illustration 14 

Evaluate the following : 
(a) $\frac{1-\text{tan}x}{1+\text{tan}x}$ (b) $\frac{\text{sec}x}{\text{log}\left( \text{sec}x+\text{tan}x \right)}$ 
(c) $\frac{1}{x+x\text{log}x}$ (d) $\frac{\text{a}}{\text{b}+\text{ce}{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}}$ 

Solution: 

All the above question are of the type $\frac{{{f}'}}{f}$. Let us see how
(a) $\int \frac{1-\text{tan}x}{1+\text{tan}x}dx$
Right now it is not in ${f}'/f$ form. But if we put $\text{tan}x=\text{sin}x/\text{cos}x$
$\therefore \int \frac{1-\text{tan}x}{1+\text{tan}x}dx=\int \frac{1-\frac{\text{sin}x}{\text{cos}x}}{1+\frac{\text{sin}x}{\text{cos}x}}=\int \frac{\text{cos}x-\text{sin}x}{\text{cos}x+\text{sin}x}$
Now take another look at the last integral, do you see $\text{{f}'}/\text{f}$ form
Bingo, yes we $\text{can}\frac{d}{dx}\left( \text{cos}x+\text{sin}x \right)=\text{cos}x-\text{sin}x$
$\therefore \int \frac{1-\text{tan}x}{1+\text{tan}x}dx=\text{log}\left| \text{cos}x+\text{sin}x \right|+c$
(b) $\int \frac{\text{sec}x}{\text{log}\left( \text{sec}x+\text{tan}x \right)}dx$
$\frac{d}{dx}\left( \text{log}\left( \text{sec}x+\text{tan}x \right)=\frac{1}{\left( \text{sec}x+\text{tan}x \right)}\left( \text{sec}x\text{tan}x+\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x \right)=\text{sec}x \right.$
$\therefore $ integral $\int \frac{\text{sec}xdx}{\text{log}\left( \text{sec}x+\text{tan}x \right)}=\text{log}\left| \text{log}\left( \text{sec}x+\text{tan}x \right) \right|+c$
(c) $\int \frac{1}{x+\text{log}x}=\int \frac{1dx}{x\left( 1+\text{log}x \right)}=\int \frac{1/x}{\left( 1+\text{log}x \right)}dx$
Now, $\frac{\text{d}}{\text{dx}}\left( 1+\text{logx} \right)=\left( \frac{1}{\text{x}} \right)$
$\therefore \int \frac{1d\text{ d}}{\text{x}+\text{xlogx}}=\text{log}\left| \text{x}+\text{xlogx} \right|+\text{c}$
(d) $\int \frac{\text{a}}{\text{b}+\text{c}{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}}=\int \frac{\text{a}}{{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}\left( \text{b}{{\text{e}}^{-\text{x}}}+\text{c} \right)}$ (taking ${{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}$ common $)$
$=\int \frac{a{{e}^{-x}}}{\left( b{{e}^{-x}}+c \right)}=\left( \frac{a}{b} \right)\int \frac{b{{e}^{-x}}}{b{{e}^{-x}}+c}=\left( \frac{-a}{b} \right)\int \frac{-b{{e}^{-x}}}{b{{e}^{-x}}+c}$
Now $\frac{d}{dx}\left( b{{e}^{-x}}+c \right)=-b{{e}^{-x}}$
$\therefore \int \frac{a}{b+c{{e}^{-x}}}=\text{log}\left| b{{e}^{-x}}+c \right|$

Illustration 15 

Evaluate: 

(a) $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}-1}{x\left( {{x}^{2}}+1 \right)}dx$ 
(b) $\int \frac{{{x}^{e-1}}+{{e}^{x-1}}}{{{x}^{e}}+{{e}^{x}}}dx$ 
(c) $\int \frac{\text{sin}2xdx}{a\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x+b\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}$ 
(d) $\int \frac{dx}{x+\sqrt{x}}dx$ 

Solution: 

(a) $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}-1}{x\left( {{x}^{2}}+1 \right)}dx$
at first sight you will not be able to judge that its a $\frac{{\text{{f}'}}}{\text{f}}$ type question.
$\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}-1}{x\left( {{x}^{2}}+1 \right)}dx=\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}-1}{{{x}^{2}}\left( x+\frac{1}{x} \right)}dx=\int \frac{1-\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}{\left( x+\frac{1}{x} \right)}dx$
now $\text{{f}'}/\text{f}$ type is evident
$\frac{d}{dx}\left( x+\frac{1}{x} \right)=1-\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}$
$\therefore $ $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}-1}{x\left( {{x}^{2}}+1 \right)}dx=\text{log}\left| x+\frac{1}{x} \right|+c$
(b) $\frac{{{\text{x}}^{\text{e}-1}}+{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}-1}}}{{{\text{x}}^{\text{e}}}+{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}}$
at first look this gives the feel of $\text{{f}'}/\text{f}$ type but $\frac{d{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}}{\text{dx}}\ne {{\text{e}}^{\text{x}-1}}$, this is a block ade here.
$\frac{d}{dx}\left( {{x}^{e}}+{{e}^{x}} \right)=e{{x}^{e-1}}+{{e}^{x}}$
which we can get by multiplying the numerator e, thus integral becomes
$\therefore \int \frac{{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}+\text{e}{{\text{x}}^{\text{e}-1}}}{{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}+{{\text{x}}^{\text{e}}}}\left( \frac{1}{\text{e}} \right)\text{dx}=\frac{1}{\text{e}}\text{log}\left| {{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}+{{\text{x}}^{\text{e}}} \right|+\text{c}$
(c) $\int \frac{\text{sin}2x}{a\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x+b\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}dx$
$\frac{d}{dx}\left( a\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x+b\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x \right)$ $=2a\text{cos}x\left( -\text{sin}x \right)+2b\text{sin}x\left( \text{cos}x \right)$
$=-a\left( 2\text{sin}x\text{cos}x \right)+b\left( 2\text{sin}x\text{cos}x \right)$
$=\left( b-a \right)\text{sin}2x$
& we have sin $2\text{x}$ in numerator, for $\left( \text{b}-\text{a} \right)$ multiplying & dividing by $\left( \text{b}-\text{a} \right)$.
$\therefore \int \frac{\text{sin}2xdx}{a\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x+b\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}=\frac{1}{\left( b-a \right)}\text{log}\left| a\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x+b\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x \right|+c$
(d) $\int \frac{\text{dx}}{\text{x}+\sqrt{\text{x}}}$
In this question students start substituting values like $\text{x}={{\text{t}}^{2}}$ etc., which is though also correct. But let us see how without substituting such values we can cover this in ${f}'/f$ type directly.
$\int \frac{dx}{x+\sqrt{x}}=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x}\left( \sqrt{x}+1 \right)}dx=2\int \frac{1/2\sqrt{x}}{\left( \sqrt{x}+1 \right)}dx$
Now $\frac{d}{dx}\left( \sqrt{x}+1 \right)=\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{x}$
$\int \frac{dx}{x+\sqrt{x}}=2\text{log}\left| \sqrt{x}+1 \right|+c$

Type II $\int {{[f\left( x \right)]}^{n}}{f}'\left( x \right)dx$ 

if we put $f\left( x \right)=t$, then
${f}'\left( x \right)dx=dt$, hence integral converts to
$\int ~{{t}^{n}}dt=\frac{{{t}^{n+1}}}{n+1}+c$
$\therefore \int ~{{[f\left( x \right)]}^{n}}{f}'\left( x \right)dx=\frac{f{{(x)}^{n+1}}}{n+1}+c$
Let us take some examples.

Illustration 16 
(a) $\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}x\text{tan}xdx$
(b) $\int x\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}{{x}^{2}}\text{sin}{{x}^{2}}dx$ 
(c) $\frac{1}{2\sqrt{x}}\text{tan}\sqrt{x}\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\sqrt{x}$
(d) $\int \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{3}}x\text{cos}xdx$ 
(e) $\int {{x}^{2}}{{\left( {{x}^{3}}+1 \right)}^{10}}dx$ 

Solution: 

(a) $\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}x\text{tan}xdx$
We can write this as $\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{3}}x\left( \text{sec}x\text{tan}x \right)dx$ and $\frac{d}{dx}\left( \text{sec}x \right)=\text{sec}x\text{tan}x$ hence applying the above formula
$\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}x\text{tan}xdx=\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}x}{4}+c$
(b) $\int x\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}{{x}^{2}}\text{sin}{{x}^{2}}dx$
$=\int \left( x\text{sin}{{x}^{2}} \right)\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}{{x}^{2}}dx$
$\frac{d}{dx}\left( \text{cos}{{x}^{2}} \right)=\left( \text{sin}{{x}^{2}} \right)\left( 2x \right)$
$\therefore \int x\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}{{x}^{2}}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}dx=\frac{1}{2}\left( \frac{\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{4}}{{x}^{2}}}{4} \right)+c$
(c) $\int \frac{1}{2\sqrt{\text{x}}}\text{tan}\sqrt{\text{x}}\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\sqrt{\text{x}}\text{dx}$
$\frac{d}{dx}\left( \text{tan}\sqrt{x} \right)=\left( \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\sqrt{x} \right)\left( \frac{1}{2\sqrt{x}} \right)$
$\therefore \int \frac{1}{2 \sqrt{\mathrm{x}}} \tan \sqrt{\mathrm{x}} \sec ^{2} \sqrt{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{\tan ^{2} \sqrt{\mathrm{x}}}{2}$
(d) $\int \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{3}}x\text{cos}xdx$
$\frac{d}{dx}\left( \text{sin}x \right)=\text{cos}x$
$\therefore \int \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{3}}x\text{cos}xdx=\frac{\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{4}}x}{4}+c$ 

 

${{\int{{{x}^{2}}({{x}^{3}}+1)}}^{10}}dx=\frac{1}{3}{{\int{({{x}^{3}}+1)}}^{10}}(3{{x}^{2}}dx)$
$=\frac{1}{3}\int ~{{\text{u}}^{10}}\text{du}=\frac{1}{3}\cdot \frac{{{\text{u}}^{11}}}{11}+\text{C}=\frac{1}{33}{{\left( {{\text{x}}^{3}}+1 \right)}^{11}}+\text{C}$

concepts.title: Substitution done in cases where the integrand is in the form of f[g(x)].g'(x)
concepts.conceptBody:
Type-3 $\int \text{f}\left[ \text{g}\left( \text{x} \right) \right]\text{{g}'}\left( \text{x} \right)\text{dx}$ 

In this type, we substitute $\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)=\text{t}$, then

${g}'\left( x \right)dx=dt$

Hence integral reduces to $\int f\left( t \right)dt$

NOTE : For this substitution $\text{g}\left( \text{x} \right)$ should be continuous & differentiable function.

Q) Evaluate: 

(a) $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}}{1+{{x}^{6}}}dx$ 
(b) $\int \sqrt{\frac{\text{x}}{{{\text{a}}^{3}}-{{\text{x}}^{3}}}}dx$ 
(c) $\int \left( \frac{{{x}^{24}}}{{{x}^{10}}+1} \right)dx$ 
(d) $\int \frac{{{\text{e}}^{\text{ata}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x}}}}{1+{{\text{x}}^{2}}}\text{dx}$ 

Solution: 

(a) $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}}{1+{{x}^{6}}}dx$

see here $\left( 1+{{x}^{6}} \right)$ can be written as $\left( 1+{{\left( {{x}^{3}} \right)}^{2}} \right)$ and we know $\frac{d}{dx}\left( {{x}^{3}} \right)=3{{x}^{2}}$ which is present in numerator.

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Substituting ${{\text{x}}^{3}}=\text{t}$

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & ~\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }3{{\text{x}}^{2}}\text{dx}=\text{dt} \\ {} & ~\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{x}}^{2}}\text{dx}=\frac{\text{dt}}{3} \\ \end{array}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ integral is, $\int \left( \frac{1}{3} \right)\frac{\text{dt}}{1+{{\text{t}}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{3}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{t}+\text{c}$

$=\frac{1}{3}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}{{\text{x}}^{3}}+\text{c}$

(b) $\int \sqrt{\frac{x}{\left( {{a}^{3}}-{{x}^{3}} \right)}dx}=\int \frac{{{x}^{1/2}}dx}{\sqrt{{{\left( {{a}^{3/2}} \right)}^{2}}-{{\left( {{x}^{3/2}} \right)}^{2}}}}$

now here let ${{\text{x}}^{3/2}}=\text{t}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\frac{3}{2}{{\text{x}}^{1/2}}\text{dx}=\text{dt}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{x}^{1/2}}dx=\frac{2}{3}dt$, which is numerator only.

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ integral is $\int \left( \frac{2}{3} \right)\frac{\text{dt}}{\sqrt{{{\left( {{\text{a}}^{3/2}} \right)}^{2}}-{{\text{t}}^{2}}}}$

using standard formula for $\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}t$

$=\frac{2}{3}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{\text{t}}{{{\text{a}}^{3/2}}}=\left( \frac{2}{3} \right)\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{{{\text{x}}^{3/2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{3/2}}} \right)+\text{c}$

(c) $\int \frac{{{\text{x}}^{24}}}{{{\text{x}}^{10}}+1}\text{dx}=\int \frac{{{\text{x}}^{24}}\text{dx}}{{{\left( {{\text{x}}^{5}} \right)}^{2}}+1}=\int \frac{\left( {{\text{x}}^{5\times 4}} \right){{\text{x}}^{4}}}{{{\left( {{\text{x}}^{5}} \right)}^{2}}+1}\text{dx}$

Now let ${{\text{x}}^{5}}=\text{t}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }5{{\text{x}}^{4}}\text{dx}=\text{dt};\therefore $ integral becomes

$~\int ~\frac{{{\text{t}}^{4}}d\text{t}}{5\left( {{\text{t}}^{2}}+1 \right)}=\frac{1}{5}\int ~\left( \frac{{{\text{t}}^{4}}}{{{\text{t}}^{2}}+1} \right)dt=\frac{1}{5}\int ~\frac{{{\text{t}}^{4}}-1+1}{{{\text{t}}^{2}}+1}dt$

$~=\frac{1}{5}\left[ \int ~\frac{{{\text{t}}^{4}}-1}{{{\text{t}}^{2}}+1}+\int ~\frac{1}{{{\text{t}}^{2}}+1}\text{dt} \right]=\frac{1}{5}\left[ \int ~\frac{\left( {{\text{t}}^{2}}-1 \right)\left( {{\text{t}}^{2}}+1 \right)}{{{\text{t}}^{2}}+1}\text{dt}+\int ~\left( \frac{1}{{{\text{t}}^{2}}+1} \right)d\text{t} \right]$

$~=\frac{1}{5}\left[ \int ~\left( {{\text{t}}^{2}}-1 \right)\text{dt}+\int ~\frac{1}{{{\text{t}}^{2}}+1}\text{dt} \right]=\frac{1}{5}\left[ \frac{{{\text{t}}^{3}}}{3}-\text{t}+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{t} \right]$

putting the value of $t$ in the integral

$=\frac{1}{5}\left[ \frac{{{x}^{15}}}{3}-{{x}^{5}}+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}{{x}^{5}} \right]+c$

(d) [Here ${{e}^{\text{ata}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x}}$ is a function of $\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x$ and d.c. of $\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x=\frac{1}{1+{{x}^{2}}}=$ second function.

Hence put $\text{z}=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x}$

Let $z=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x$, then $dz=\frac{1}{1+{{x}^{2}}}dx$

Now $\int \frac{{{\text{e}}^{\text{atan}-1}}\text{x}}{1+{{\text{x}}^{2}}}\text{dx}=\int {{\text{e}}^{\text{az}}}\text{dz}=\frac{{{\text{e}}^{\text{az}}}}{\text{a}}+\text{c}=\frac{1}{\text{a}}{{\text{e}}^{\text{ata}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x}}}+\text{c}$

concepts.title: Substitution done in cases where the integrand is assuming the form of f(x).[(ax+b)^n]
concepts.conceptBody:
Substitution done in cases where the integrand is assuming the form of f(x).[(ax+b)^n]

$\text{P}\left( \text{x} \right)\cdot {{(\text{ax}+\text{b})}^{\text{n}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ or }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\frac{\text{P}\left( \text{x} \right)}{{{(\text{ax}+\text{b})}^{\text{n}}}}$

where $\text{P}\left( \text{x} \right)$ is a polynomial in $\text{x}$ and $\text{n}$ is a positive rational number.

Working Rule: 

Put $z=ax+b$

Q) Evaluate $\int \left( 5x+3 \right)\sqrt{2x-1}dx$. 

Solution: 

$\left[ {} \right.$ Here $P\left( x \right)=5x+3$ and $\sqrt{2x-1}$ is of the form $\left. {{(ax+b)}^{n}} \right]$.

Let $\text{z}=2\text{x}-1$ then $\frac{\text{dz}}{\text{dx}}=2,\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\therefore \text{dx}=\frac{\text{dz}}{2}$

Again $\because \text{z}=2\text{x}-1;\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\therefore \text{x}=\frac{\text{z}+1}{2}$

Now $\int \left( 5x+3 \right)\sqrt{2x-1}dx$

$=\int ~\left[ 5\left( \frac{\text{z}+1}{2} \right)+3 \right]\sqrt{\text{z}}\frac{\text{dx}}{2}$

$=\int ~\frac{5\text{z}+11}{4}\sqrt{\text{z}}\text{dz}=\int ~\left( \frac{5}{4}{{\text{z}}^{3/2}}+\frac{11}{4}\sqrt{\text{z}} \right)\text{dz}$

$=\int \frac{5 z+11}{4} \sqrt{z} d z=\int\left(\frac{5}{4} z^{\frac{2}{3}}+\frac{11}{4} \sqrt{z}\right) d z$

$=\frac{5}{4}\int ~{{\text{z}}^{\frac{3}{2}}}\text{dx}+\frac{11}{4}\int ~\sqrt{\text{z}}\text{dz}$

$=\frac{5}{4}\frac{{{\text{z}}^{\frac{5}{2}}}}{\frac{5}{2}}+\frac{11}{4}\cdot \frac{{{\text{z}}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}{\frac{3}{2}}+\text{c}=\frac{1}{2}{{\text{z}}^{\frac{5}{2}}}+\frac{11}{6}{{\text{z}}^{\frac{3}{2}}}+\text{c}$

$=\frac{{{z}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}{2}\left( z+\frac{11}{3} \right)+c=\frac{{{z}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}{2}\left( \frac{3z+11}{3} \right)+c$

Now putting back the value of $\text{z}$ in terms of $\text{x}$.

$~=\frac{{{(2\text{x}-1)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}{6}\cdot \left[ 3\left( 2\text{x}-1 \right)+11 \right]+\text{c}$

$~=\frac{{{(2\text{x}-1)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}{6}\cdot \left( 6\text{x}+8 \right)+\text{c}=\frac{{{(2\text{x}-1)}^{\frac{3}{2}}}\cdot \left( 3\text{x}+4 \right)}{3}+\text{c}$

Note: This problem can also be solved by the method of transformation.

Q) Evaluate $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{(a+bx)}^{2}}}dx$ 

Solution: 

$\left[ {} \right.$ Here $\text{P}\left( \text{x} \right)={{\text{x}}^{2}}$ and ${{(\text{a}+\text{bx})}^{2}}$ is of the form $\left. {{(\text{ax}+\text{b})}^{\text{n}}} \right]$

Let $\text{z}=\text{a}+\text{bx }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\therefore \frac{\text{dz}}{\text{dx}}=\text{b }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\therefore \text{dx}=\frac{\text{dz}}{\text{b}}$

Again $\because \text{z}=\text{a}+\text{bx }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\therefore \text{x}=\frac{\text{z}-\text{a}}{\text{b}}$

Now $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{(a+bx)}^{2}}}dx=\int \frac{{{\left( \frac{z-a}{b} \right)}^{2}}}{{{z}^{2}}}\cdot \frac{dz}{b}=\int \frac{{{z}^{2}}-2az+{{a}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}{{z}^{2}}}\frac{1}{b}dz$

$=\frac{1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{b}}^{3}}}\int ~\left( \frac{{{\text{z}}^{2}}}{{{\text{z}}^{2}}}-\frac{2\text{az}}{{{\text{z}}^{2}}}+\frac{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}{{{\text{z}}^{2}}} \right)\text{dz}=\frac{1}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{b}}^{3}}}\int ~\left( 1-\frac{2\text{a}}{\text{z}}+{{\text{a}}^{2}}{{\text{z}}^{2}} \right)\text{dz}$

$=\frac{1}{{{b}^{3}}}\left[ \int dz-2a\int \frac{dz}{z}+{{a}^{2}}\int {{z}^{-2}}dz \right]$

$=\frac{1}{{{b}^{3}}}\left[ z-2a\text{log}\left| z \right|+{{a}^{2}}\frac{{{z}^{-1}}}{-1} \right]+c$

$=\frac{1}{{{b}^{3}}}\left[ z-2a\text{log}\left| z \right|-\frac{{{a}^{2}}}{z} \right]+c$

$=\frac{1}{{{b}^{3}}}\left[ a+bx-2a\text{log}\left| a+bx \right|-\frac{{{a}^{2}}}{a+bx} \right]+c$

$=\frac{1}{{{b}^{3}}}\left[ bx-2a\text{log}\left| a+bx \right|-\frac{{{a}^{2}}}{a+bx} \right]+{c}'$

( where $\left. {c}'=c+\frac{a}{{{b}^{3}}} \right)$ )

concepts.title: Substitution done in cases when the argument of the trigonometric functions present in the integrand is itself a function of x
concepts.conceptBody:
Substitution done in cases when the argument of the trigonometric functions present in the integrand is itself a function of x

Working Rule: 

If integrand contains any expression of the form $\text{sin}\theta ,\text{cos}\theta ,\text{tan}\theta ,\text{cot}\theta ,\text{sec}\theta ,\text{cosec}\theta $, where $\theta $ is a function of $\text{x}$, then put $\text{z}=\theta $.

Worked out Examples 

Q) $\begin{array}{*{35}{l}} ~(a)~Find~\int \frac{sin\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}}dx & ~ (b)~\int \frac{cot\left( logx \right)}{x}dx~~ (c)~\int \frac{{{e}^{x}}\left( 1+x \right)}{co{{s}^{2}}\left( x{{e}^{x}} \right)}dx \\ \end{array}$

Solution: 

(a) [Here integrand contains $\text{sin}\sqrt{\text{x}}$ which is of the form of $\text{sin}\theta $, where $\theta =\sqrt{\text{x}}$ which is a function of $\text{x}$, therefore, put $\text{z}=\sqrt{\text{x}}]$

Let $z=z=\sqrt{x}$ then $dz=\frac{1}{2\sqrt{x}}dx$

or $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ dx}=2\sqrt{\text{x}}\text{dz}=2\text{zdz }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left[ \because \text{z}=\sqrt{\text{x}} \right]$

Now $\int \frac{\text{sin}\sqrt{x}}{\sqrt{x}}dx=\int \frac{\text{sin}z}{z}2zdz=2\int \text{sin}zdz$

$=2\left( -\text{cos}z \right)+c=-2\text{cos}\sqrt{x}+c$

(b) [Here integrand contains expression of the form $\text{cot}\theta $, where $\theta =\text{logx}$ is a function of $\text{x}$, therefore, put $\text{z}=\text{logx}]$

Let $\text{z}=\text{logx}$ then $\text{dz}=\frac{1}{\text{x}}\text{dx}$

Now $\int \frac{\text{cot}\left( \text{log}x \right)}{x}dx\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }=\int \text{cot}zdz$

$=\text{log}\left| \text{sin}z \right|+c$ $~\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left[ \therefore \text{cot}xdx=\text{log}\left| \text{sin}x \right| \right]$

$~=\text{log}\left| \text{sin}\left( \text{log}x \right) \right|+c.$

(c) [Here integrand contains expression of the form $\text{cos}\theta $, where $\theta =\text{x}{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}$ is a function of $\text{x}$, therefore, put $\left. \text{z}=\text{x}{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}} \right]$ Let $\text{z}=\text{x}{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}$, then $\text{dz}=\left( 1\cdot {{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}+\text{x}{{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}} \right)\text{dx}={{\text{e}}^{\text{x}}}\left( 1+\text{x} \right)\text{dx}$

Now $\int \frac{{{e}^{x}}\left( 1+x \right)}{\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\left( x{{e}^{x}} \right)}dx=\int \frac{dz\left( 1+x \right)}{\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}z}=\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}zdz$

$=\text{tan}z+c=\text{tan}\left( x{{e}^{x}} \right)+c.$

concepts.title: Substitution done in cases when the integrand is in the form of [(sin x)^m][(cos x)^n]
concepts.conceptBody:
Trigonometric Integral 

Integration of the form: 

$\int ~\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{m}}x\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{n}}xdx$

Working Rule: 

(i) If power of $\text{sinx}$ is odd positive integer, put $\text{z}=\text{cosx}$

(ii) If power of $\text{cosx}$ is odd positive integer, put $\text{z}=\text{sinx}$

(iii) If powers of both $\text{sinx}$ and $\text{cosx}$ are odd positive integers, put $\text{z}=\text{sinx}$ or $\text{z}=\text{cosx}$.

(iv) If power of neither $\text{cosx}$ nor $\text{sinx}$ is odd positive integer, see the sum of powers of $\text{sinx}$ and $\text{cosx}$.

(a) If the sum of powers is even negative integer, put $z=\text{tan}x$.

(b) If the sum of powers $\left( m+n \right)$ is even positive integer and $m,n$ are integers, express the integrand as the algebraic sum of sines and cosines of multiple angles.

For this use the following formulae whichever is needed.

In this case method of transformation is used in place of method of substitution

$\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x=\frac{1-\text{cos}2x}{2},\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x=\frac{1+\text{cos}2x}{2}$

$\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{3}}x=\frac{3\text{sin}x-\text{sin}3x}{4},\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}x=\frac{3\text{cos}x+\text{cos}3x}{4}$

$2\text{sin}x\text{cos}x=\text{sin}2x$

Q) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ (a) }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Find }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\int \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{5}}xdx$ $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ (b) }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\int \text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}x\sqrt{\text{sin}x}dx$ 

(c) $\int \frac{dx}{\text{sin}x\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}x}$ (d) $\int \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{4}}xdx$ 

Solution: 

(a) [Here power of $\text{sinx}$ is 5 which is odd positive integer, therefore, put $\text{z}=\text{cosx}$ ]

Let $\text{z}=\text{cosx}$ then $\text{dz}=-\text{sinxdx}$

Now $\int \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{5}}xdx\int \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{4}}x\text{sin}xdx=\int {{\left( \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x \right)}^{2}}\text{sin}xdx$

$=\int {{\left( 1-\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x \right)}^{2}}\text{sin}xdx$

$=\int {{\left( 1-{{\text{z}}^{2}} \right)}^{2}}\left( -\text{dz} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left[ \therefore \text{z}=\text{cosx} \right]$

$=\int \left( 1-2{{z}^{2}}+{{z}^{4}} \right)dz=-\left[ z-2\frac{{{z}^{3}}}{3}+\frac{{{x}^{5}}}{5} \right]+c$

$=\text{z}+\frac{2}{3}\cdot {{\text{z}}^{3}}-\frac{{{\text{z}}^{5}}}{5}+\text{c}$

$=-\text{cosx}+\frac{2}{3}\cdot \text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}\text{x}-\frac{\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{5}}\text{x}}{5}+\text{c}$

(b) [Here power of $\text{cos}x$ is 3 which is odd positive integer, therefore, put $z=\text{sin}x]$

Let $\text{z}=\text{sinx}$, then $\text{dz}=\text{cosx}d\text{x}$

Now $\int \text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}x\sqrt{\text{sin}x}dx=\int \text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x\sqrt{\text{sin}x}\text{cos}xdx$

$=\int ~\left( 1-\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x \right)\sqrt{\text{sin}x}\text{cos}xdx$

$=\int \left( 1-{{z}^{2}} \right)\sqrt{z}dz=\int \left( \sqrt{z}-{{z}^{\frac{5}{2}}} \right)dz$

$=\frac{{{z}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}{\frac{3}{2}}-\frac{{{z}^{\frac{7}{2}}}}{\frac{7}{2}}+c=\frac{2}{3}{{z}^{\frac{3}{2}}}-\frac{2}{7}{{z}^{\frac{7}{2}}}+c$

$=\frac{2}{3}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{\frac{3}{2}}}\text{x}-\frac{2}{7}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{\frac{7}{2}}}\text{x}+\text{c}$

(c) [Here power of $\text{sin}x$ is -1 and that of $\text{cos}x$ is -3 . Since sum of powers of $\text{sin}x$ and $\text{cos}x$ is -4 which is even and negative, therefore, put $z=\text{tan}x$.]

Let $z=\text{tan}x$, then $dz=\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx$

Now $\int \frac{dx}{\text{sin}x\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}x}=\int \frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x}{\text{sin}x\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{3}}x\cdot \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x}dx=\int \frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x}{\text{sin}x\text{cos}x}dx\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left[ \therefore \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x=1 \right]$

$=\int \frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x}{\text{tan}x}\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx$

$=\int \frac{1+{{\text{z}}^{2}}}{\text{z}}\text{dz }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left[ \begin{array}{*{35}{l}} \because \text{tanx}=\text{z}=\frac{\text{z}}{1}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ and }\!\!~\!\!\text{ se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\text{xdx}=\text{dz} \\ \And \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\text{x}=1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\text{x} \\ \end{array} \right]$

$=\int \frac{1+{{\text{z}}^{2}}}{\text{z}}\text{dz}=\int \left( \frac{1}{\text{z}}+\text{z} \right)\text{dz}$

$=\text{log}\left| z \right|+\frac{{{z}^{2}}}{2}+c$

$=\text{log}\left| \text{tan}x \right|+\frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}{2}+c$

(d) [Here power of $\text{sin}x$ is 4 and that of $\text{cos}x$ is 0 and sum of their powers is 4 which is even positive integer.

Therefore, we will have to express $\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{4}}x$ as sines and cosines of multiple angles.]

Now $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\int \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{4}}\text{xdx}=\int {{\left( \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\text{x} \right)}^{2}}\text{dx}=\int {{\left( \frac{1-\text{cos}2\text{x}}{2} \right)}^{2}}\text{dx}$

$=\frac{1}{4}\int \left( 1-2\text{cos}2x+\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}2x \right)dx$

$=\frac{1}{4}\left[ \int dx-2\int \text{cos}2xdx+\int \text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}2xdx \right]$

$=\frac{1}{4}\left[ \text{x}-2\cdot \frac{\text{sin}2\text{x}}{2}+\int \frac{1+\text{cos}4\text{x}}{2}\text{dx} \right]$

$=\frac{1}{4}\left[ x-\text{sin}2x+\frac{1}{2}\left( x+\frac{\text{sin}4x}{4} \right) \right]+c$

$=\frac{1}{4}\left[ \frac{3}{2}x-\text{sin}2x+\frac{\text{sin}4x}{8} \right]+c$

$=\frac{3}{8}\text{x}-\frac{\text{sin}2\text{x}}{4}+\frac{\text{sin}4\text{x}}{32}+\text{c}$

concepts.title: Substitution done in cases when the integrand is in the form of [(tan x)^m][(sec x)^n] or [(cot x)^m][(cosec x)^n]
concepts.conceptBody:
Integrals of the form : 

$\int \text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{m}}x\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{n}}xdx\text{ or }\int \text{co}{{\text{t}}^{m}}x\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{n}}xdx$s

Working Rule: 

I . For $\int ~\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{m}}x\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{n}}xdx$

  1. If power of secx is even positive integer, put $\text{z}=\text{tanx}$.
  2. If power of secx is not even positive integer, then see the power of $\text{tan}x$.
  3. If power of tanx is odd positive integer, put $z=$ secx.
  4. If power of tanx is even positive integer, then put $\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x-1$ in place of $\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x$ and then substitute $\text{z}=\text{tanx}$.
  5. If power of tanx is zero and power of secx is odd positive integer greater than 1, then method of integration by parts is used.

For example : $\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{3}}xdx,\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{5}}xdx$ etc.

II . For $\int \text{co}{{\text{t}}^{\text{m}}}\text{xcose}{{\text{c}}^{\text{n}}}\text{xdx}$

  •  
    1. If power of cosecx is even positive integer put $\text{z}=\text{cotx}$.
    2. If power of cosecx is not even positive integer, see the power of cotx.
      • If power of cotx is odd positive integer, put $\text{z}=$ cosecx.
      • If power of cotx is even positive integer, put $\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x-1$ in place of $\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{2}}x$ and then substitute $\text{z}=$ cotx.
    3. If power of cotx is zero and power of cosecx is odd positive integer greater than 1 , then method of integration by parts is used.

For example : $\int \text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{3}}xdx,\int \text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{5}}xdx$ 

Illustration 23 

(a) Find $\int \text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{n}}x\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx,\left( n\ne -1 \right)$
(b) $\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}xdx$ 
(c) $\int \text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{3}}xdx$
(d) $\int \text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{4}}xdx$ 
(e) $\int \text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{6}}xdx$ 

Solution: 

(a) [Here power of secx is 2 which is even positive integer, therefore, put $z=\text{tan}x$] Let $\text{z}=\text{tanx}$, then $\text{dz}=\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\text{xdx}$
Now $\int ~\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{\text{n}}}\text{xse}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\text{xdx}=\int ~{{\text{z}}^{\text{n}}}\text{dz}$ $~=\frac{{{\text{z}}^{\text{n}+1}}}{\text{n}+1}+\text{c }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\left[ \therefore \text{n}\ne -1 \right]$
$~=\frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{\text{n}+1}}\text{x}}{\text{n}+1}+\text{c}.$
(b) [Here power of secx is even positive integer, therefore, put $z=\text{tan}x$]
Let $\text{z}=\text{tanx}$, then $\text{dz}=\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\text{xdx}$
Now $\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}xdx=\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x\cdot \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx=\int \left( 1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x \right)\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx$
$=\int ~\left( 1+{{z}^{2}} \right)dz=z+\frac{{{z}^{3}}}{3}+c=\text{tan}x+\frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{3}}x}{3}+c.$
(c) [Here power of secx is not even positive integer and power of tanx is odd positive integer therefore, put $\text{z}=\text{sec}]$
Let $\text{z}=\text{sec}x$, then $\text{dz}=\text{sec}x\text{tanxdx}$
Now $\int \text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{3}}xdx=\int \frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x\text{sec}x\text{tan}x}{\text{sec}x}dx$
$=\int ~\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x-1}{\text{sec}x}\text{sec}x\text{tan}xdx=\int ~\frac{{{z}^{2}}-1}{z}dz$
$=\int\left(z-\frac{1}{z}\right) d z=\frac{z^{2}}{2}-\log |z|+c$
$=\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x}{2}-\text{log}\left| \text{sec}x \right|+c$
(d) [Here power of cosecx is even positive integer therefore, put $z=\text{cotx}$ ]
Let $\text{z}=\text{cotx}$, then $\text{dz}=-\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\text{xdx}$
Now $\int \text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{4}}xdx=\int \text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x\cdot \text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx$
$~=\int ~\left( 1+\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{2}}x \right)\text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx$
$~=\int ~\left( 1+{{z}^{2}} \right)\left( -dz \right)=-\int ~\left( 1+{{z}^{2}} \right)dz$
$~=-\left( z+\frac{{{z}^{3}}}{3} \right)+c=-\text{cot}x-\frac{\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{3}}x}{3}+c.$
(e) [Here power of $\text{tan}x$ is even positive integer therefore, change $\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\text{x}$ into $\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\text{x}-1$ and then put $\text{z}=\text{tanx}]$
Now $\int \text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{6}}xdx=\int {{\left( \text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x \right)}^{3}}dx=\int {{\left( \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x-1 \right)}^{3}}dx$
$~=\int ~\left( \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{6}}x-3\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}x+3\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x-1 \right)dx$
$~=\int ~\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{6}}xdx-3\int ~\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}xdx+3\int ~\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx-\int ~dx$
$=\int ~\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{6}}xdx-3\int ~\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}xdx+3\text{tan}x-x$
Let $z=\text{tan}x$ then $dz=\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx$
Now $\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{6}}xdx-3\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}xdx$
$=\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{4}}x\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx-3\int \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx$
$=\int {{\left( 1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x \right)}^{2}}\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx-3\int \left( 1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x \right)\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx$
$=\int {{\left( 1+{{z}^{2}} \right)}^{2}}dz-3\int \left( 1+{{z}^{2}} \right)dz$
$=\int {{\left( 1+2{{z}^{2}}+{{z}^{4}} \right)}^{2}}dz-3\int \left( 1+{{z}^{2}} \right)dz$
$=\text{z}+2\cdot \frac{{{\text{z}}^{3}}}{3}+\frac{{{\text{z}}^{5}}}{5}-3\text{z}-3\cdot \frac{{{\text{z}}^{3}}}{3}$
$=\frac{{{z}^{5}}}{5}-\frac{{{z}^{3}}}{3}-2z=\frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{5}}x}{5}-\frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{3}}x}{3}-2\text{tan}x$
Putting in (i), we get
$\int ~\text{tan}6xdx$ $~=\frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{5}}x}{5}-\frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{3}}x}{3}-2\text{tan}x+3\text{tan}x-x+c$
$~=\frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{5}}x}{5}-\frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{3}}x}{3}+\text{tan}x-x+c.$

concepts.title: Some Standard Substitutions
concepts.conceptBody:
Some standard substitutions 

Function of the form Substitution

1. $\sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}-{{\text{x}}^{2}}}$ or $\frac{1}{\sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}-{{\text{x}}^{2}}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ x}=\text{asin}\theta $ or $\text{acos}\theta $ 

Example : $I=\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}-{{x}^{2}}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Let $x=a\text{sin}\theta \Rightarrow dx=a\text{cos}\theta d\theta $

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ So }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int ~\frac{a\text{cos}\theta d\theta }{\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}-{{a}^{2}}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta }}=\int ~d\theta =\theta +C=\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{x}{1}+C$

Function of the form Substitution

2. $\frac{1}{\left( {{a}^{2}}+{{x}^{2}} \right)}$ or $\frac{1}{\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}+{{x}^{2}}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x=a\text{tan}\theta $ or $a\text{cot}\theta $ 

Example : $I=\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{9+{{x}^{2}}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Let $x=3\text{tan}\theta \Rightarrow dx=a\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta d\theta $

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ So }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int ~\frac{3\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta d\theta }{9\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta }=\frac{1}{3}\int ~d\theta =\frac{\theta }{3}+C=\frac{1}{3}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{x}{3}+C$

Function of the form Substitution

3. $\frac{1}{\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}-{{\text{a}}^{2}}}}$ or $\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}-{{\text{a}}^{2}}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ x}=\text{asec}\theta $ or $\text{acosec}\theta $

Example : $I=\int \frac{dx}{x\sqrt{{{x}^{4}}-1}}$

Here $\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{4}}-1}=\sqrt{{{\left( {{\text{x}}^{2}} \right)}^{2}}-1}$

Put ${{\text{x}}^{2}}=\text{sec}\theta $

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }2\text{x}d\text{x}=\text{sec}\theta \text{tan}\theta \text{d}\theta $

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ So }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int ~\frac{\text{sec}\theta \text{tan}\theta d\theta }{2\sqrt{\text{sec}\theta }\left( \sqrt{\text{sec}\theta }\cdot \sqrt{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta -1} \right)}=\int ~\frac{1}{2}d\theta =\frac{\theta }{2}+C=\frac{1}{2}\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\left( {{x}^{2}} \right)+C$

Function of the form Substitution

4. $\sqrt{\frac{a-x}{a+x}}$ or $\sqrt{\frac{a+x}{a-x}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x=a\text{cos}2\theta $

Example $:I=\int \sqrt{\frac{1-x}{1+x}}dx\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Let $x=\text{cos}2\theta \Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }dx=-2\text{sin}2\theta d\theta $

So I $~=\sqrt{\frac{1-\text{cos}2\theta }{1+\text{cos}2\theta }}\left( -2\text{sin}2\theta \right)d\theta =-2\int ~\frac{\text{sin}\theta }{\text{cos}\theta }\cdot 2\text{sin}\theta \text{cos}\theta d\theta $

$~=2\int ~2\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta d\theta =-2\int ~\left( 1-\text{cos}2\theta \right)d\theta $

$~=-2\theta +\text{sin}2\theta +C=-\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}\text{x}+\sqrt{1-{{x}^{2}}}+C$

Function of the form Substitution

5. $\sqrt{\frac{x}{a-x}}$ or $\sqrt{\frac{a-x}{x}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x=a\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta $ or $x=a\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\theta $

Example : Evaluate $\int \sqrt{\frac{{{x}^{9}}}{2{{x}^{8}}-{{x}^{9}}}}dx$

$I=\int \sqrt{\frac{{{x}^{9}}}{2{{x}^{8}}-{{x}^{9}}}}dx$

$=\int \sqrt{\frac{x}{2-x}}dx$

putting $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ x}=2\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta $

$dx=2\left( 2\text{sin}\theta \text{cos}\theta \right)d\theta $

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int \sqrt{\frac{2\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta }{2-2\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta }}2\left( 2\text{sin}\theta \text{cos}\theta \right)d\theta $

$=4\int \frac{\text{sin}\theta }{\text{cos}\theta }\text{sin}\theta \text{cos}\theta d\theta $

$=4\int \text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta d\theta =2\int 2\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta d\theta $

$=2\left( \int 1-\text{cos}2\theta d\theta \right)$

$=2\left[ \theta -\frac{\text{sin}2\theta }{2} \right]+\text{c}$

$=2\theta -\text{sin}2\theta +c$

Function of the form Substitution

6. $\sqrt{\frac{x}{a+x}}$ or $\sqrt{\frac{1+x}{x}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x=a\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta $ or $x=a\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{2}}\theta $

Example : Evaluate $\int \sqrt{\frac{x}{9+x}}dx$

$I=\int \sqrt{\frac{x}{9+x}}dx$

putting $\text{x}=9\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta $

$dx=9\left( 2\text{tan}\theta \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \right)d\theta $

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int \sqrt{\frac{9\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta }{9+9\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta }}9\left( 2\text{tan}\theta \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \right)d\theta $

$=\int \frac{\text{tan}\theta }{\sqrt{1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta }}\left( 18 \right)\text{tan}\theta \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta d\theta $

$=18\int \frac{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta d\theta }{\text{sec}\theta }$

$=18\int \text{tan}\theta \left( \text{tan}\theta \text{sec}\theta \right)d\theta $

put $\text{sec}\theta =\text{t}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ sec}\theta \text{tan}\theta d\theta =dt$

$\And $ then $\text{tan}\theta =\sqrt{{{\text{t}}^{2}}-1}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=18\int \sqrt{{{t}^{2}}-1}dt$

We will using this type later on, so we are leaving it here only. Once you have read this type do come back to solve this.

Function of the form Substitution

7. $\sqrt{\left( \frac{x-a}{b-x} \right)}$ or $\sqrt{\left( x-a \right)\left( b-x \right)}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x=a\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\theta +b\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta $

Example : Evaluate $\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{{{(x-a)}^{3}}\cdot \left( b-x \right)}}dx$ 

$\int \frac{1}{{{(x-a)}^{3}}\left( b-x \right)}dx$

putting $\text{x}=\text{aco}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\theta +\text{bsi}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta $

$dx=\left( b-a \right)\text{sin}2\theta d\theta $

$\Rightarrow I=\int \frac{2(b-a) \sin \theta \cos \theta d \theta}{\sqrt{\left(a \cos ^2 \theta+b \sin ^2 \theta\right)^3\left(b-a \cos ^2 \theta-b \sin ^2 \theta\right)}}$

$=\int \frac{2(b-a) \sin \theta \cos \theta d \theta}{\sqrt{\left(b \sin ^2 \theta-a\left(1-\cos ^2 \theta\right)\right)^3\left(b\left(1-\sin ^2 \theta\right)-a \cos ^2 \theta\right)}}$

$=\int \frac{2(b-a) \sin \theta \cos \theta d \theta}{\sqrt{\left[\left(b \sin ^2 \theta-a \sin ^2 \theta\right)^3\right]\left[b \cos ^2 \theta-a \cos ^2 \theta\right]}}$

$=\int \frac{2(b-a) \sin \theta \cos \theta d \theta}{\sqrt{(b-a)^3\left(\sin ^2 \theta\right)^3(b-a) \cos ^2 \theta}}$

$=2(b-a) \int \frac{\sin \theta \cos \theta d \theta}{(b-a)^2 \cos \theta \sin ^3 \theta}$

$=\frac{2}{(b-a)} \int \operatorname{cosec}^2 \theta d \theta=\frac{-2}{b-a} \cot \theta+c$

Function of the form Substitution

8.$\sqrt{\frac{\left( x-a \right)}{\left( x-b \right)}}$ or $\sqrt{\left( x-a \right)\left( x-b \right)}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x=a\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta -b\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta $

Example : Evaluate $\int \sqrt{\frac{x-a}{x-b}}dx$

$I=\int ~\sqrt{\frac{x-a}{x-b}}dx$

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ put }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x=a\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta -b\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta $

$\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }dx=2\left( a-b \right)\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \text{tan}\theta d\theta $

$~\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int ~\sqrt{\frac{a\left( \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta -1 \right)-b\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta }{a\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta -b\left( 1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta \right)}}2\left( a-b \right)\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \text{tan}\theta d\theta $

$=\int \sqrt{\frac{\left( a-b \right)\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta }{\left( a-b \right)\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta }}2\left( a-b \right)\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \text{tan}\theta d\theta $

$=2\int \frac{\text{tan}\theta }{\text{sec}\theta }\left( a-b \right)\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \text{tan}\theta d\theta $

$=2\left( \text{a}-\text{b} \right)\int \text{tan}\theta \left( \text{sec}\theta \text{tan}\theta \right)d\theta $

let $\text{sec}\theta =\text{t}$

$\text{sec}\theta \text{tan}\theta d\theta =dt\And \text{tan}\theta =\sqrt{{{t}^{2}}-1}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=2\left( a-b \right)\int \sqrt{{{t}^{2}}-1}dt$

We are leaving this integral here as we will cover this type in the following segments.

Function of the form Substitution

9.$\frac{1}{\sqrt{\left( x-a \right)\left( x-b \right)}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x-a={{t}^{2}}$ or $x-b={{t}^{2}}$

Example : Evaluate $\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left( x-1 \right)\left( x-2 \right)}}dx$ 

$I=\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{\left( x-1 \right)\left( x-2 \right)}}$

putting $\text{x}-2={{\text{t}}^{2}}\Rightarrow \text{dx}=2\text{t}d\text{t}$

$I=\int \frac{2tdt}{\left( {{t}^{2}}+1 \right){{t}^{2}}}=2\int \frac{dt}{\sqrt{{{t}^{2}}+1}}$

$=2\text{log}\left| t+\sqrt{{{t}^{2}}+1} \right|+c$ {do not worry about this result, we will do this one in topic some special integrals later}

Example : $\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{\left( x-1 \right)\left( x-2 \right)}}$ 

putting $\text{x}$ $~=\text{ase}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta -\text{bta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta $

$\text{dx}$ $~=2\text{asec}\theta \left( \text{sec}\theta \text{tan}\theta \right)-2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ btan}\theta \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta $

$~=2\left( \text{a}-\text{b} \right)\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \text{tan}\theta \text{d}\theta $

also note that $\text{a}=1\And \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ b}=2$ here, so putting these values also

$I$ $~=\int ~\frac{-2\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \text{tan}\theta d\theta }{\sqrt{\left( \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta -2\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta -1 \right)\left( \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta -2\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta -2 \right)}}$

$~=\int ~\frac{-2\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \text{tan}\theta d\theta }{\sqrt{\left( -\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\theta \right)\left( -\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \right)}}$

$~=\int ~\frac{-2\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\theta \text{tan}\theta }{\text{tan}\theta \text{sec}\theta }d\theta $

$~=-2\int ~\text{sec}\theta d\theta =-2\text{log}\left| \text{sec}\theta +\text{tan}\theta \right|+c$

concepts.title: Solving Integrals where the integrand is in the form of 1/(ax^2+bx+c),1/√(ax^2+bx+c) or √(ax^2+bx+c)
concepts.conceptBody:
Integration rational & irrational functions

Integral of the form $\int \frac{dx}{a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c},\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c}}$ and $\int \sqrt{a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c}dx$

For evaluating such integral we make the coefficients of ${{x}^{2}}$ in $a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c$ as one. Complete the square by adding and subtracting the square of half of the coefficient of $\text{x}$ to get the form

$\text{a}\left[ {{\left( \text{x}+\frac{\text{b}}{2\text{a}} \right)}^{2}}+\left( \frac{\text{c}}{\text{a}}-\frac{{{\text{b}}^{2}}}{4{{\text{a}}^{2}}} \right) \right]$

Q) Evaluate: 

(i) $\int \frac{dx}{{{x}^{2}}+4x+7}$ (ii) $\int \frac{dx}{1-6x-9{{x}^{2}}}$ 

(iii) $\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{9+8x-{{x}^{2}}}}$ (iv) $\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{2{{x}^{2}}+3x-2}}$ 

(v) $\int \sqrt{1-4x-{{x}^{2}}}dx$ (vi) $\int \sqrt{2{{x}^{2}}+3x+4}dx$ 

Solution: 

(i) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int \frac{dx}{{{x}^{2}}+4x+7}=\int \frac{dx}{\left( {{x}^{2}}+4x+4 \right)+\left( 7-4 \right)}$

[Adding and subtracting square of half of coefficient of $\text{x}$ ]

$~=\int ~\frac{dx}{{{(x+2)}^{2}}+3}=\int ~\frac{dx}{{{(x+2)}^{2}}+{{(\sqrt{3})}^{2}}}$

$~=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x+2}{\sqrt{3}} \right)+c$

(ii) $I=\int \frac{\text{dx}}{1-6\text{x}-9{{\text{x}}^{2}}}=-\frac{1}{9}\int \frac{\text{dx}}{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+\frac{2}{3}\text{x}-\frac{1}{9}}$

$=-\frac{1}{9}\int ~\frac{dx}{{{\left( {{x}^{2}}+\frac{2}{3}x+\frac{1}{9} \right)}^{2}}-\frac{1}{9}-\frac{1}{9}}$

[Adding and subtracting square of half of coefficient of $\text{x}$ ]

$=\frac{1}{9}\int \frac{\text{dx}}{{{\left( \text{x}+\frac{1}{3} \right)}^{2}}-\frac{2}{9}}=\frac{1}{9}\int \frac{\text{dx}}{{{\left( \text{x}+\frac{1}{3} \right)}^{2}}-{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \right)}^{2}}}$

$=\frac{1}{9}\int \frac{\text{dx}}{{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{2}}{3} \right)}^{2}}-{{\left( \text{x}+\frac{1}{3} \right)}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{9}\cdot \frac{1}{\frac{2\sqrt{2}}{3}}\text{log}\left| \frac{\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}+\text{x}+\frac{1}{3}}{\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}-\text{x}-\frac{1}{3}} \right|+\text{c}$

$=\frac{1}{6\sqrt{2}}\text{log}\left| \frac{\sqrt{2}+3x+1}{\sqrt{2}-3x-1} \right|+c$

(iii) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{9+8x-{{x}^{2}}}}dx=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{-\left[ {{x}^{2}}-8x-9 \right]}}dx$

$=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{-\left[ {{\left( {{x}^{2}}-8x+16 \right)}^{2}}-9-16 \right]}}dx=\int \frac{1}{-\left[ {{(x-4)}^{2}}-25 \right]}dx$

$=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{25-{{(x-4)}^{2}}}}dx=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{{{(5)}^{2}}-{{(x-4)}^{2}}}}dx=\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x-4}{5} \right)+c$

(iv) $\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{2{{x}^{2}}+3x-2}}=\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{2\left( {{x}^{2}}+\frac{3}{2}x-1 \right)}}$

$=\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{2}\sqrt{\left( {{x}^{2}}+\frac{3}{2}x-1 \right)}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{\left( {{x}^{2}}+\frac{3}{2}x+\frac{9}{16} \right)-1-\frac{9}{16}}}$

$=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\int \frac{\text{dx}}{\sqrt{{{\left( {{\text{x}}^{2}}+\frac{3}{4} \right)}^{2}}-\frac{25}{16}}}$

$=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{{{\left( {{x}^{2}}+\frac{3}{4} \right)}^{2}}-{{\left( \frac{5}{4} \right)}^{2}}}}$

$=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\text{log}\left| \text{x}+\frac{3}{4}+\sqrt{{{\left( \text{x}+\frac{3}{4} \right)}^{2}}-{{\left( \frac{5}{4} \right)}^{2}}} \right|+\text{c}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\text{log}\left| \frac{4\text{x}+3}{4}+\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+\frac{3}{2}\text{x}-1} \right|+\text{c}$

(v) $\int \sqrt{1-4\text{x}-{{\text{x}}^{2}}}\text{dx}$

$=\int \sqrt{-\left[ {{x}^{2}}+4x-1 \right]}dx=\int \sqrt{-\left[ {{x}^{2}}+4x+4-1-4 \right]}dx$

$=\int \sqrt{\left. -{{[x+2)}^{2}}-5 \right]}dx=\int \sqrt{5-{{(x+2)}^{2}}}dx$

$=\frac{\text{x}+2}{2}\sqrt{5-{{(\text{x}+2)}^{2}}}+\frac{5}{2}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{\text{x}+2}{\sqrt{5}} \right)+\text{c}$

$=\frac{x+2}{2}\sqrt{1-4x-{{x}^{2}}}+\frac{5}{2}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x+2}{\sqrt{5}} \right)+c$

concepts.title: Solving Integrals where the integrand is in the form of (px + q)/(ax^2+bx+c),(px+q)/√(ax^2+bx+c) or (px+q)[√(ax^2+bx+c)]
concepts.conceptBody:
Integrals of the form

$\int \frac{px+q}{a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c}dx,\int \frac{px+q}{\sqrt{a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c}}dx$ and $\left( px+q \right)\sqrt{a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c}dx$

For evaluating such integrals, we choose suitable constants A and B such that

$px+q=A\left[ \frac{d}{dx}\left( a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c \right) \right]+B$

$\text{A}$ and $\text{B}$ can be determined by equating the coefficient of $\text{x}$ and the constant terms on both sides. This enables us to separate the given integral into two integrals.
 

Q) (i) $\frac{\text{x}+3}{\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+4\text{x}+5}}\text{dx}$
(ii) $\int \left( 3\text{x}-2 \right)\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+\text{x}+1}$
(iii) $\frac{{{x}^{2}}+5x+3}{{{x}^{2}}+3x+2}dx$

Solution: 

(i) $I=\int \frac{\text{x}+3}{\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+4\text{x}+5}}\text{dx}$
Putting $x+3=A\left[ \frac{d}{dx}\left( {{x}^{2}}+4x+5 \right) \right]+B$
$\Rightarrow \text{x}+3=\text{A}\left( 2\text{x}+4 \right)+\text{B}$
Comparing the coefficient of $x$, we get
$\text{I}=2\text{ A }\Rightarrow \text{ A}=1/2$
Comparing the constants, we get
3$=4\text{ A}+\text{B } \Rightarrow  3=2+\text{B} \Rightarrow  \text{B}=1$
$\therefore $I = $\int{\frac{1/2(2x+4)+1}{\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+4x+5}}dx}$ [Using (i)]
= $\frac{1}{2}\int{\frac{2x+4}{\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+4\text{x}+5}}dx+\int{\frac{1}{\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+4\text{x}+5}}dx}}$
Putting ${{\text{x}}^{2}}+4\text{x}+5=\text{t}$ in 1st integral $\Rightarrow \left( 2\text{x}+4 \right)\text{dx}=\text{dt}$
I$=\frac{1}{2}\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{t}}dt+\int \frac{1}{{{(x+2)}^{2}}+1}dx$
$=\frac{1}{2}\cdot 2\sqrt{t}+\text{log}\left| x+2+\sqrt{{{(x+2)}^{2}}+1} \right|+c$
$=\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+4x+5}+\text{log}\mid x+2+\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+4x+5\mid }+c$
(iii) $\text{I}=\int \left( 3\text{x}-2 \right)\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+\text{x}+1}\text{dx}$
Let $3\text{x}-2=\text{A}\left[ \frac{\text{d}}{\text{dx}}\left( {{\text{x}}^{2}}+\text{x}+1 \right) \right]+\text{B}$
$\Rightarrow 3\text{x}-2=\text{A}\left( 2\text{x}+1 \right)+\text{B}$
Comparing the coefficient of $\text{x}$, we get
$3=2\text{ A }\Rightarrow \text{ A}=\frac{3}{2}$
Comparing the constant terms
$-2=\text{A}+\text{B} \Rightarrow  -2=\frac{3}{2}+\text{B }$
$\Rightarrow \text{ B}=\frac{-7}{2}$
$\therefore I=\int\left[\frac{3}{2}(2 x+1)-\frac{7}{2}\right] \sqrt{x^2+x+1} d x$
$=\frac{3}{2} \int(2  \mathrm{x}+1) \sqrt{\mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{x}+1} \mathrm{dx}-\frac{7}{2} \int \sqrt{\mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{x}+1} \mathrm{dx}$
Putting ${{\text{x}}^{2}}+\text{x}+1=\text{t}$ in 1st integral
$\Rightarrow \left( 2\text{x}+1 \right)\text{dx}=\text{dt}$
$I=\int \frac{3}{2}\int \sqrt{t}dt-\frac{7}{2}\int \sqrt{{{\left( x+\frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}+1-\frac{1}{4}dx}=\frac{3}{2}\cdot \frac{2}{3}\cdot {{t}^{3/2}}-\frac{7}{2}\int \sqrt{{{\left( x+\frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \right)}^{2}}}dx$
$={{\left( {{x}^{2}}+x+1 \right)}^{3/2}}-\frac{7}{2}\left[ \frac{x+\frac{1}{2}}{2}\sqrt{{{\left( x+\frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \right)}^{2}}}+3/8\frac{3}{\frac{4}{2}}\text{log}\left| x+\frac{1}{2}+\sqrt{{{\left( x+\frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \right)}^{2}}} \right|+c \right]$
$={{\left( {{x}^{2}}+x+1 \right)}^{3/2}}-\frac{7\left( 2x+1 \right)}{8}\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+x+1}-\frac{21}{16}\text{log}\left| x+\frac{1}{2}+\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+x+1} \right|+c$

 

(iv) $I=\frac{{{x}^{2}}+5x+3}{{{x}^{2}}+3x+2}dx$
I$=\int 1+\frac{2x+1}{{{x}^{2}}+3x+2}dx=\int 1dx+\int \frac{2x+1}{{{x}^{2}}+3x+2}$
$=x+\int \frac{2x+1}{{{x}^{2}}+3x+2}dx$
Let $2x+1=A\left[ \frac{d}{dx}\left( {{x}^{2}}+3x+2 \right) \right]+B$
$\Rightarrow 2\text{x}+1=\text{A}\left( 2\text{x}+3 \right)+\text{B}$
Comparing the coefficient of $\text{x}$, we get
$2=2\text{ A }\Rightarrow \text{ A}=1$
Comparing the constant terms, we get
1$=3A+B$ $\Rightarrow$ B = -2 $\left[ \because A=1 \right]$
$\therefore$ I $=x+\int \frac{1\left( 2x+3 \right)-2}{{{x}^{2}}+3x+2}dx=x+\int \frac{2x+3}{{{x}^{2}}+3x+2}dx-2\int \frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}+3x+2}dx$
$=x+\text{log}\left| {{x}^{2}}+3x+2 \right|-2\int \frac{1}{{{\left( x+\frac{3}{2} \right)}^{2}}-{{\left( \frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}}dx$
=$x+\text{log}\left| {{x}^{2}}+3x+2 \right|-2\cdot \frac{1}{2\cdot \frac{1}{2}}\text{log}\left| \frac{x+\frac{3}{2}-\frac{1}{2}}{2} \right|+c$
=$x+\text{log}\left| {{x}^{2}}+3x+2 \right|-2\text{log}\left| \frac{x+1}{2} \right|+c$

concepts.title: Solving Integrals where the integrand is in the form of (px^2+qx+r)/√ (ax^2+bx+c)
concepts.conceptBody:
Integrals of the Form : $\int \frac{p{{x}^{2}}+qx+r}{\sqrt{a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c}}dx$ 

For evaluating such integrals, we choose suitable constants, A, B and C such that$p{{x}^{2}}+qx+r=A\left( a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c \right)+B\left( \frac{d}{dx}\left( a{{x}^{2}}+bx+c \right)+C \right.$

$\text{A},\text{B}$ and $\text{C}$ can be determined by equating the coefficients of ${{\text{x}}^{2}},\text{x}$ and constant terms respectively on both sides.

This enables us to separate the given integral into three integrals which are easily integrable.

Illustration 49 

Evaluate : 

(i) $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}+3x+6}{\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+x+1}}dx$ (ii) $\int \frac{5-4\text{x}-2{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{\sqrt{1-\text{x}-{{\text{x}}^{2}}}}\text{dx}$ 

(iii) $\int \frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}-2}{\sqrt{3-{{\text{x}}^{2}}}}dx$ 

Solution : 

(i) $I=\int \frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+3\text{x}+6}{\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+\text{x}+1}}\text{dx}$

Let ${{x}^{2}}+3x+6=A\left( {{x}^{2}}+x+1 \right)B\left( \frac{d}{dx}\left( {{x}^{2}}+x+1 \right)+c \right.$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{x}}^{2}}+3\text{x}+6=\text{A}\left( {{\text{x}}^{2}}+\text{x}+1 \right)+\text{B}\left( 2\text{x}+1 \right)+\text{c}$

Comparing the coefficient of ${{\text{x}}^{2}}$, we get

$1=\text{A}$

Comparing the coefficient of $\text{x}$, we get

$3=\text{A}+2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ B }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ B}=2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ B}=1$

Comparing the constant terms, we get

$6$ $=A+B+C$ $\Rightarrow $ $~\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }C=4$

$\therefore $I $~=\int ~\frac{\left( {{x}^{2}}+x+1 \right)+\left( 2x+1 \right)+4}{\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+x+1}}dx$

$~=\int ~\frac{{{x}^{2}}+x+1}{\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+x+1}}dx+\int ~\frac{2x+1}{\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+x+1}}dx+4\int ~\frac{1}{\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+x+1}}dx$

$\left. =\int ~\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+x+1}dx+\int ~\frac{1}{\sqrt{t}}dt+4\int ~\frac{1}{\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+x+1}}dx\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\![\!\!\text{ where }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }t={{x}^{2}}+x+1 \right]$

$=\int \sqrt{\left(x+\frac{1}{2}\right){2}+\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right){2}} d x+2 \sqrt{t}+4 \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{\left(x+\frac{1}{2}\right){2}+\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right){2}}} d x$ $=\frac{x+\frac{1}{2}}{2}\sqrt{{{\left( x+\frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \right)}^{2}}}+\frac{{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \right)}^{2}}}{2}\text{log}\left| x+\frac{1}{2}+\sqrt{{{\left( x+\frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \right)}^{2}}} \right|$ $+2 \sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}+\mathrm{x}+1}+\frac{3}{8} \log \left|\mathrm{x}+\frac{1}{2}+\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^{2}+\mathrm{x}+1}\right|+\mathrm{c}$

$=\frac{2\text{x}+9}{4}\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+\text{x}+1}+\frac{35}{8}\text{log}\left| \text{x}+\frac{1}{2}+\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+\text{x}+1} \right|+\text{c}$

(ii) $I=\int \frac{5-4\text{x}-2{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{\sqrt{1-\text{x}-{{\text{x}}^{2}}}}\text{dx}$

Let $5-4\text{x}-2{{\text{x}}^{2}}=\text{A}\left( 1-\text{x}-{{\text{x}}^{2}} \right)+\text{B}\left[ \frac{\text{d}}{\text{dx}}\left( 1-\text{x}-{{\text{x}}^{2}} \right) \right]+\text{C}$

$\Rightarrow 5-4\text{x}-2{{\text{x}}^{2}}=\text{A}\left( 1-\text{x}-{{\text{x}}^{2}} \right)+\text{B}\left( -1-2\text{x} \right)+\text{C}$

Comparing the coefficients of ${{\text{x}}^{2}}$, we get

$-2=-\text{A }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A}=2$

Comparing the coefficient of $x$, we get

$-4=-\text{A}-2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ B }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }-2=-2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ B }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ B}=1$

Comparing the constant terms, we get

5 = A – B + C $\Rightarrow $ C = 4

$\therefore $I$~=\int ~\frac{2\left( 1-x-{{x}^{2}} \right)+\left( -1-2x \right)+4}{\sqrt{1-x-{{x}^{2}}}}dx$

$~=2\int ~\frac{1-x-{{x}^{2}}}{\sqrt{1-x-{{x}^{2}}}}dx+\int ~\frac{-1-2x}{\sqrt{1-x-{{x}^{2}}}}dx+4\int ~\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x-{{x}^{2}}}}dx$

$~=2\int ~\sqrt{1-x-{{x}^{2}}}dx+\int ~\frac{-1-2x}{\sqrt{1-x-{{x}^{2}}}}dx+4\int ~\frac{1}{\sqrt{1-x-{{x}^{2}}}}dx$

$=2\int ~\sqrt{{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{5}}{2} \right)}^{2}}-{{\left( x+\frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}}dx+\int ~\frac{1}{\sqrt{t}}dt+4\int ~\frac{1}{\sqrt{{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{5}}{2} \right)}^{2}}-{{\left( x+\frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}}}dx\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\!\![\!\!\text{ where }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }t=1-x-{{x}^{2}}$]

$=2\left[\frac{\mathrm{x}+\frac{1}{2}}{2} \sqrt{\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}\right){2}-\left(\mathrm{x}+\frac{1}{2}\right){2}}+\frac{\left(\frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}\right)^{2}}{2} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}+\frac{1}{2}}{\frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}}\right)\right]+2 \sqrt{1-\mathrm{x}-\mathrm{x}^{2}}+4 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}+\frac{1}{2}}{\frac{\sqrt{5}}{2}}\right)+\mathrm{c}$ $=\frac{2x+1}{2}\sqrt{1-x-{{x}^{2}}}+\frac{5}{4}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{2x+1}{\sqrt{5}} \right)+2\sqrt{1-x-{{x}^{2}}}+4\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{2x+1}{\sqrt{5}} \right)+c$ $=\frac{2 x+5}{2} \sqrt{1-x-x^{2}}+\frac{21}{4} \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x+1}{\sqrt{5}}\right)+c$

(iii) $I=\int \frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}-2}{\sqrt{3-{{\text{x}}^{2}}}}\text{dx}$

Let ${{\text{x}}^{2}}-2=\text{A}\left( 3-{{\text{x}}^{2}} \right)+\text{B}\left[ \frac{\text{d}}{\text{dx}}\left( 3-{{\text{x}}^{2}} \right) \right]+\text{c}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{x}}^{2}}-2=\text{A}\left( 3-{{\text{x}}^{2}} \right)+\text{B}\left( -2\text{x} \right)+\text{c}$

Comparing the coefficients of ${{\text{x}}^{2}}$, we get

$1=-\text{A }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A}=-1$

Comparing the coefficient of $\text{x}$, we get

$0=-2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ B }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ B}=0$

Comparing the constant terms, we get

$-2=3\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A}+\text{C }\Rightarrow \text{C}=-2-3\left( -1 \right)=1$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int \frac{-\left( 3-{{x}^{2}} \right)+1}{\sqrt{3-{{x}^{2}}}}dx=\int \frac{-\left( 3-{{x}^{2}} \right)}{\sqrt{3-{{x}^{2}}}}dx+\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{3-{{x}^{2}}}}dx$

$=\int -\sqrt{3-{{x}^{2}}}dx+\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{3-{{x}^{2}}}}dx$

$=-\int \sqrt{{{(\sqrt{3})}^{2}}-{{x}^{2}}}dx+\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{{{(\sqrt{3})}^{2}}-{{x}^{2}}}}dx$

$=-\left[ \frac{x}{2}\sqrt{3-{{x}^{2}}}+\frac{3}{2}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x}{\sqrt{3}} \right) \right]+\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}+c$

$=-\frac{x}{2}\sqrt{3-{{x}^{2}}}-\frac{3}{2}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x}{\sqrt{3}} \right)+\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{x}{\sqrt{3}}+c$

$=-\frac{x}{2}\sqrt{3-{{x}^{2}}}-\frac{1}{2}\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x}{\sqrt{3}} \right)+c$

concepts.title: Solving Integrals where the integrand is in the form of (x^2±1)/(x^4+kx^2+1)
concepts.conceptBody:
Integrals of the form : $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}\pm 1}{{{x}^{4}}+k{{x}^{2}}+1}dx$ 

For evaluating such integrals, divide the numerator and denominator by ${{\text{x}}^{2}}$. Complete the square of denominator to get the form ${{\left( \text{x}+\frac{1}{\text{x}} \right)}^{2}}+\text{a}$ or ${{\left( \text{x}-\frac{1}{\text{x}} \right)}^{2}}+\text{a}$

Then the integral can be evaluated by using the method of substitution.

Q) Evaluate: 

(i) $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}+1}{{{x}^{4}}+1}dx$ (ii) $\int \frac{1}{{{x}^{4}}+1}dx$ 

(iii) $\int \frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}-1}{{{\text{x}}^{4}}+{{\text{x}}^{2}}+1}dx$ (iv) $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}-3x+1}{{{x}^{4}}+{{x}^{2}}+1}dx$ 

Solution: 

(i) $\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}+1}{{{x}^{4}}+1}dx$

Dividing numerator and denominator by ${{\text{x}}^{2}}$, we get

$I=\int \frac{1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}{{{x}^{2}}+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}dx=\int \frac{1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}{\left( {{x}^{2}}+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}-2 \right)+2}dx=\int \frac{1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}{{{\left( x-\frac{1}{x} \right)}^{2}}+{{(\sqrt{2})}^{2}}}dx$

putting $x-\frac{1}{x}=t\Rightarrow \left( 1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}} \right)dx=dt$

$I=\int \frac{dt}{{{t}^{2}}+{{(\sqrt{2})}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{t}{\sqrt{2}}+c=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x-\frac{1}{x}}{\sqrt{2}} \right)+c$

$=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{{{x}^{2}}-1}{\sqrt{2}x} \right)+c$

(ii) $I=\int \frac{1}{{{\text{x}}^{4}}+1}\text{dx}=\frac{1}{2}\int \frac{2}{{{\text{x}}^{4}}+1}\text{dx}$

$=\frac{1}{2}\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}+1-{{x}^{2}}+1}{{{x}^{4}}+1}dx=\frac{1}{2}\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}+1}{{{x}^{4}}+1}-\frac{{{x}^{2}}-1}{{{x}^{4}}+1}dx$

$=\frac{1}{2}\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}+1}{{{x}^{4}}+1}dx-\frac{1}{2}\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}-1}{{{x}^{4}}+1}dx$

Now proceed as in part (i) to get

$I=\frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{{{x}^{2}}-1}{\sqrt{2}x} \right)-\frac{1}{4\sqrt{2}}\text{log}\left| \frac{{{x}^{2}}+1-\sqrt{2}x}{{{x}^{2}}+1+\sqrt{2}x} \right|+c$

(iii) $I=\int \frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}-1}{{{\text{x}}^{4}}+{{\text{x}}^{2}}+1}\text{dx}$

Dividing numerator and denominator by ${{\text{x}}^{2}}$, we get

$I=\int \frac{1-\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}{{{x}^{2}}+1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}dx=\int \frac{1-\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}{\left( {{x}^{2}}+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}+2 \right)-1}dx=\int \frac{1-\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}{{{\left( x+\frac{1}{x} \right)}^{2}}-1}dx$

Putting $x+\frac{1}{x}=t\Rightarrow \left( 1-\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}} \right)dx=dt$

$I=\int \frac{dt}{{{t}^{2}}-1}=\frac{1}{2}\text{log}\left| \frac{t-1}{t+1} \right|+c=\frac{1}{2}\text{log}\left| \frac{x+\frac{1}{x}-1}{x+\frac{1}{x}+1} \right|+c=\frac{1}{2}\text{log}\left| \frac{{{x}^{2}}+1-x}{{{x}^{2}}+1+x} \right|+c$

(iv) $I=\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}-3x+1}{{{x}^{4}}+{{x}^{2}}+1}dx=\int \frac{{{x}^{2}}+1}{{{x}^{4}}+{{x}^{2}}+1}dx-3\int \frac{x}{{{x}^{4}}+{{x}^{2}}+1}dx$

Dividing numerator and denominator by ${{\text{x}}^{2}}$ in 1 st integral and

Putting ${{\text{x}}^{2}}=\text{t}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }2\text{xdx}=\text{dt}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ xdx}=\frac{1}{2}\text{dt}$ in 2nd integral.

$I=\int \frac{1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}{{{x}^{2}}+1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}dt-\frac{3}{2}\int \frac{dt}{{{t}^{2}}+t+1}$

$=\int \frac{1+\frac{1}{{{x}^{2}}}}{{{\left( x-\frac{1}{x} \right)}^{2}}+3}dx-\frac{3}{2}\int \frac{dt}{{{\left( t+\frac{1}{2} \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \right)}^{2}}}$

Putting $\text{z}=\text{x}-\frac{1}{\text{x}}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ dz}=\left( 1+\frac{1}{{{\text{x}}^{2}}} \right)\text{dx}$ in 1st integral

$I=\int \frac{dz}{{{z}^{2}}+{{(\sqrt{3})}^{2}}}-\frac{3}{2}\frac{1}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left[ \frac{\text{t}+\frac{1}{2}}{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}} \right]+\text{c}$

$=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{\text{z}}{\sqrt{3}}-\sqrt{3}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{2\text{t}+1}{\sqrt{3}} \right)+\text{c}$

$=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{\text{x}-\frac{1}{\text{x}}}{\sqrt{3}} \right)-\sqrt{3}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{2{{\text{x}}^{2}}+1}{\sqrt{3}} \right)+\text{c}$

$=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}-1}{\sqrt{3}\text{x}} \right)-\sqrt{3}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{2{{\text{x}}^{2}}+1}{\sqrt{3}} \right)+\text{c}$

concepts.title: Solving integrals where the integrand is in the form of 1/P√ Q where P and Q are either linear or quadratic functions in x
concepts.conceptBody:
Integrals of the form : $\int \frac{dx}{P\sqrt{Q}}$  where $\mathbf{P}$ and $\mathbf{Q}$ are linear or quadratic expression in $\text{x}$ 

Integrals of this form can be of three types:

  1. $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ Q}$ is linear and $\text{P}$ is linear or quadratic. For evaluating such integrals, put $\text{Q}={{\text{t}}^{2}}$
  2. $\text{Q}$ is quadratic and $\text{P}$ is linear. For evaluating such integrals, put $\text{P}=\frac{1}{\text{t}}$
  3. Both $\text{P}$ and $\text{Q}$ are pure quadratic.

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ For evaluating such integrals, put $\text{x}=\frac{1}{\text{t}}$.

Q) Evaluate : 
(i) $\int \frac{dx}{\left( x+2 \right)\sqrt{x-1}}$ 
(ii) $\int \frac{\sqrt{\text{x}}}{1+\text{x}}\text{dx}$ 
(iii) $\int \frac{dx}{\left( {{x}^{2}}+1 \right)\sqrt{x}}$ 

Solution: 

(i) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ I}=\int \frac{\text{dx}}{\left( \text{x}+2 \right)\sqrt{\text{x}-1}}$

Here $\text{P}$ is linear and $\text{Q}$ is also linear i.e., type 1 .

Putting $\text{x}-1={{\text{t}}^{2}}\Rightarrow \text{x}={{\text{t}}^{2}}+1\Rightarrow \text{dx}=2\text{t}d\text{t}$

$I=\int \frac{2tdt}{\left( {{t}^{2}}+3 \right)\sqrt{{{t}^{2}}}}=\int \frac{2}{{{t}^{2}}+{{(\sqrt{3})}^{2}}}dt=\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{t}{\sqrt{3}}+c=\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{\sqrt{x-1}}{\sqrt{3}}+c$

(ii) $I=\int \frac{\sqrt{\text{x}}}{1+\text{x}}\text{dx}=\int \frac{\text{x}}{\left( 1+\text{x} \right)\sqrt{\text{x}}}\text{dx}$

$=\int \frac{\left( 1+x \right)-1}{\left( 1+x \right)\sqrt{x}}dx=\int \frac{1+x}{\left( 1+x \right)\sqrt{x}}-\frac{1}{\left( 1+x \right)\sqrt{x}}dx$

$=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}-\frac{1}{\left( 1+x \right)\sqrt{x}}dx=\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}dx-\int \frac{1}{\left( 1+x \right)\sqrt{x}}dx$

$=2\sqrt{\text{x}}-\int \frac{1}{\left( 1+\text{x} \right)\sqrt{\text{x}}}$

Putting $\text{x}={{\text{t}}^{2}}\Rightarrow \text{dx}=2\text{t}d\text{t}$

I $~=2\sqrt{x}-\int ~\frac{2tdt}{\left( 1+{{t}^{2}} \right)\sqrt{{{t}^{2}}}}=2\sqrt{x}-\int ~\frac{2}{1+{{t}^{2}}}dt$

$~=2\sqrt{x}-2\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}t+c=2\sqrt{x}-2\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\sqrt{x}+c$

(iii) $\text{I}=\int \frac{\text{dx}}{\left( {{\text{x}}^{2}}+1 \right)\sqrt{\text{x}}}$

Putting $\text{x}={{\text{t}}^{2}}\Rightarrow \text{dx}=2\text{t}d\text{t}$

$I=\int ~\frac{2tdt}{\left( {{t}^{4}}+1 \right)\sqrt{{{t}^{2}}}}=\int ~\frac{2}{{{t}^{4}}+1}dt=\int ~\frac{{{t}^{2}}+1-{{t}^{2}}+1}{{{t}^{4}}+1}dt$

$~=\int ~\frac{{{t}^{2}}+1}{{{t}^{4}}+1}dt-\int ~\frac{{{t}^{2}}-1}{{{t}^{4}}+1}dt=\int ~\frac{1+\frac{1}{{{t}^{2}}}}{{{t}^{2}}+\frac{1}{{{t}^{2}}}}dt-\int ~\frac{1-\frac{1}{{{t}^{2}}}}{{{t}^{2}}+\frac{1}{{{t}^{2}}}}dt$

$~=\int ~\frac{1+\frac{1}{{{t}^{2}}}}{{{\left( t-\frac{1}{t} \right)}^{2}}+2}dt-\int ~\frac{1-\frac{1}{{{t}^{2}}}}{{{\left( t+\frac{1}{t} \right)}^{2}}-2}dt$

Putting $\text{t}-\frac{1}{\text{t}}=\text{z}$ in 1st integral Putting $t+\frac{1}{t}=y$ in 2nd integral

to get $\left( 1+\frac{1}{{{t}^{2}}} \right)dt=dz$ to get $\left( 1-\frac{1}{{{t}^{2}}} \right)dt=dy$

$I=\int \frac{dz}{{{z}^{2}}+{{(\sqrt{2})}^{2}}}-\int \frac{dy}{{{y}^{2}}-{{(\sqrt{2})}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{z}{\sqrt{2}}-\frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}\text{log}\left| \frac{y-\sqrt{2}}{y+\sqrt{2}} \right|+c$

$=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{t-\frac{1}{t}}{\sqrt{2}}-\frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}\text{log}\left| \frac{t+\frac{1}{t}-\sqrt{2}}{t+\frac{1}{t}+\sqrt{2}} \right|+c$

$=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{{{\text{t}}^{2}}-1}{\sqrt{2}\text{t}}-\frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}\text{log}\left| \frac{{{\text{t}}^{2}}+1-\sqrt{2}\text{t}}{{{\text{t}}^{2}}+1+\sqrt{2}\text{t}} \right|+\text{c}$

$=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{\text{x}-1}{\sqrt{2}\text{x}} \right)-\frac{1}{2\sqrt{2}}\text{log}\left| \frac{\text{x}+1-\sqrt{2}\text{x}}{\text{x}+1+\sqrt{2}\text{x}} \right|+\text{c}$

Q) Evaluate $:\int \frac{dx}{\left( x+2 \right)\sqrt{{{x}^{2}}+6x+7}}$ 

Solution: 

$I=\int \frac{d x}{(x+2) \sqrt{x^2+6 x+7}}$

Putting $x+2=\frac{1}{t} \Rightarrow x=\frac{1}{t}-2 \Rightarrow d x=-\frac{1}{t^2} d t$

$I=\int \frac{-\frac{1}{t^2} d t}{\frac{1}{t} \sqrt{\left(\frac{1}{t}-2\right)^2+6\left(\frac{1}{t}-2\right)+7}}=\int \frac{-\frac{1}{t} d t}{\sqrt{\frac{1}{t^2}-\frac{4}{t}+4+\frac{6}{t}-12+7}}$

$=\int \frac{-\frac{1}{t} d t}{\sqrt{\frac{1}{t^2}+\frac{2}{t}-1}}=\int \frac{-\frac{1}{t} d t}{\sqrt{1+2 t-t^2}}=-\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{1+2 t-t^2}}$

$=-\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{2-(t-1)^2}}=-\int \frac{d t}{\sqrt{(\sqrt{2})^2-(t-1)^2}}=-\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{t-1}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+c$

$\left.=-\sin ^{-1} \frac{\mathrm{c}\left(\frac{1}{x+2}-1\right.}{\sqrt{2}}\right)+c=-\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{-x-1}{\sqrt{2}(x+2)}\right)+c=\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+1}{\sqrt{2}(x+2)}\right)+c$

concepts.title: Working Rule to solve the integrals of various irrational functions
concepts.conceptBody:
Integration of Irrational Functions 

Types of functions (intergrand) approach 

1. $f\left\{ x,{{\left( \frac{ax+b}{cx+d} \right)}^{a/n}} \right\}\left( a,b,c,d,\alpha ,n\in \mathbf{R} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Substitute $:\frac{ax+b}{cx+d}={{t}^{n}}$ 

Example : Evaluate $\int {{\left( \frac{\text{x}+2}{2\text{x}+3} \right)}^{1/2}}\cdot \frac{1}{\text{x}}\text{dx}$

Solution : Substitute $\frac{x+2}{2x+3}={{t}^{2}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }x=\left( \frac{3{{t}^{2}}-2}{1-2{{t}^{2}}} \right)\Rightarrow dx=\frac{-2tdt}{{{\left( 1-2{{t}^{2}} \right)}^{2}}}$ $\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=-2\int t\cdot \left( \frac{1-2{{t}^{2}}}{3{{t}^{2}}-2} \right)\left( \frac{-2tdt}{\left( 1-2{{t}^{2}} \right)} \right)=4\int \frac{{{t}^{2}}dt}{\left( 3{{t}^{2}}-2 \right)\left( 1-2{{t}^{2}} \right)}$

Let ${{\text{t}}^{2}}=\text{y}$

Now using partial fraction $\frac{\text{y}}{\left( 3\text{y}-2 \right)\left( 1-2\text{y} \right)}=\frac{\text{A}}{\left( 3\text{y}-2 \right)}+\frac{\text{B}}{\left( 1-2\text{y} \right)}$, we get A and $\text{B}$ and then we evaluate the integration with respect to $t$ after replacing y by ${{t}^{2}}$.

Substitute: 

2. $f\left\{ x,{{(ax+b)}^{a/n}},{{(ax+b)}^{\beta /m}} \right\}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ $ax+b={{t}^{p}}$, where $p$ is L.C.M. of $m$ and $n$. 

Example : Evaluate $\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt{\left( x+1 \right)}-{{(x+1)}^{1/4}}}$

Solution : Substitute $\text{x}+1={{\text{t}}^{4}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }(\because \text{p}$ is 4$)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ dx}=4{{\text{t}}^{3}}\text{dt}$

$\therefore $ I $~=\int ~\left( \frac{4{{t}^{3}}dt}{{{t}^{2}}-t} \right)=4\int ~\left( \frac{{{t}^{2}}}{t-1} \right)dt=4\int ~\left( \frac{{{t}^{2}}-1+1}{t-1} \right)dt$

$~=4\int ~\left( t+1 \right)dt+4\int ~\frac{1}{t-1}dt=4\cdot \frac{{{t}^{2}}}{2}+4t+4\text{log}\left| t-1 \right|+C$

Type V 

3. $f\left\{ {{\left( x\pm \sqrt{{{a}^{2}}+{{x}^{2}}} \right)}^{n}} \right\}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Work rule $:x\pm \sqrt{{{a}^{2}}+{{x}^{2}}}=t$ 

Example : Evaluate $\int {{\left( x+\sqrt{{{a}^{2}}+{{x}^{2}}} \right)}^{2}}dx$

Solution : Substitute $\text{x}+\sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}+{{\text{x}}^{2}}}=\text{t }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ x}=\left( \frac{{{\text{a}}^{2}}-{{\text{t}}^{2}}}{2\text{t}} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{dx}=\left( \frac{1}{2}+\frac{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}{2{{\text{t}}^{2}}} \right)\text{dt}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ I}=\int ~\left( \frac{{{\text{t}}^{2}}}{2}+\frac{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}{2} \right)\text{dt}$

4. $\sqrt{\frac{dx}{{{x}^{m}}{{(a+bx)}^{p}}}};m+p\in N,m+p>1\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ Work rule $:a+bx=tx$ 

Example : Evaluate $\int \frac{\text{dx}}{{{\text{x}}^{2/3}}{{(2+3\text{x})}^{4/3}}}$

Solution : Substitute $2+3\text{x}=\text{tx }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ dx}=\frac{-2}{{{(\text{t}-3)}^{2}}}\text{dt}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int ~\frac{\frac{-2}{{{(t-3)}^{2}}}dt}{{{\left( \frac{2t}{t-3} \right)}^{4/3}}}=-\int ~\frac{dt}{2.{{t}^{4/3}}}$

Now you can evaluate it easily.

5. $\sqrt{\frac{dx}{{{\left\{ {{\mathbf{L}}_{1}}\left( x \right) \right\}}^{m}}{{\left\{ {{\mathbf{L}}_{2}}\left( x \right) \right\}}^{\text{n}}}}}$ Work rule 

(i) If $\text{n}>\text{m};\frac{{{\text{L}}_{1}}\left( \text{x} \right)}{{{\text{L}}_{2}}\left( \text{x} \right)}=\text{t}$

(ii) If $\text{n}<\text{m};\frac{{{\text{L}}_{2}}\left( \text{x} \right)}{{{\text{L}}_{1}}\left( \text{x} \right)}=\text{t}$

6. ${{x}^{m}}{{\left( a+b{{x}^{n}} \right)}^{p}}dx$ Work rule 

(i) If $p\in I$, substitute $x={{t}^{S}}$ where $\text{s}$ is L.C.M. of denominator of $\text{m}\And \text{n}$.

(ii) If $\frac{\text{m}+1}{\text{n}}$ is an Integer, substitute $\text{a}+b{{\text{x}}^{\text{n}}}={{\text{t}}^{\text{s}}}$ is the denominator of fraction $\text{p}$.

(iii) If $\frac{\text{m}+1}{\text{n}}+\text{p}$ substitute $a{{\text{x}}^{-\text{n}}}+\text{b}={{\text{t}}^{\text{s}}}$ $\text{s}$ is denominator of rational number $\text{p}$.

Q) Find the value of 
(a) $\int \frac{x+\sqrt[3]{{{x}^{2}}}+\sqrt[6]{x}}{x\left( 1+\sqrt[3]{x} \right)}dx$ (b) $\int \frac{\text{dx}}{\sqrt{\text{x}}\cdot {{\left( {{\text{x}}^{1/4}}+1 \right)}^{10}}}$ 
(c) $\int \frac{dx}{\sqrt[4]{{{(x-1)}^{3}}{{(x+2)}^{5}}}}$ 

Solution: 

(a) Here L.C.M. of 3 and 6 is 6.

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ we put $\text{x}={{\text{t}}^{6}}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ dx}=6{{\text{t}}^{5}}\text{dt}$

Hence $I=6\cdot \int \frac{\left( {{t}^{6}}+{{t}^{4}}+t \right)}{{{t}^{6}}\left( 1+{{t}^{2}} \right)}{{t}^{5}}dt=6\cdot \int \frac{{{t}^{5}}+{{t}^{3}}+1}{\left( 1+{{t}^{2}} \right)}dt=6\cdot \int {{t}^{3}}dt+6\cdot \int \frac{dt}{1+{{t}^{2}}}$

$=\frac{3}{2}{{\text{t}}^{4}}+6\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{t}+\text{C}=\frac{3}{2}{{\text{x}}^{4/6}}+6\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}{{\text{x}}^{1/6}}+\text{C}$

(b) Hence $\alpha $ is integer $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\therefore $ put $\text{x}={{\text{t}}^{4}}$ $\Rightarrow $ $\text{dx}=4{{\text{t}}^{3}}\text{dt}$

I $~=\int ~\frac{4{{t}^{3}}dt}{{{t}^{2}}{{(t+1)}^{10}}}$

$~=4\int ~\frac{t}{{{(t+1)}^{10}}}dt=4\int ~\frac{\left( t+1 \right)-1}{{{(t+1)}^{10}}}dt=4\int ~{{(t+1)}^{-9}}dt-4\int ~{{(t+1)}^{-10}}dt$

$~=-\frac{4}{8}{{(t+1)}^{-8}}+\frac{4}{9}{{(t+1)}^{-9}}+C$

$~=-\frac{1}{2}\frac{1}{{{(\sqrt[4]{x}+1)}^{8}}}+\frac{4}{9{{(\sqrt[4]{x}+1)}^{9}}}+C$

(c) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ I}=\int \frac{\text{dx}}{{{(\text{x}-1)}^{3/4}}\cdot {{(\text{x}+2)}^{5/4}}}$

$=\int ~\frac{dx}{{{\left( \frac{x-1}{x+2} \right)}^{3/4}}{{(x+2)}^{2}}}$ Put $\frac{x-1}{x+2}=t$

$\Rightarrow \frac{3}{{{(x+2)}^{2}}}dx=dt$

I $~=\frac{1}{3}\int ~{{t}^{-3/4}}dt=\frac{4}{3}{{t}^{1/4}}+C=\frac{4}{3}{{\left( \frac{x-1}{x+2} \right)}^{1/4}}+C$

concepts.title: Solving Trigonometric Integrals where the integrand is in the form of 1/(a+bcosx),1/(a+bsinx) or 1/(a+bcosx+csinx)
concepts.conceptBody:
Solving Trigonometric Integrals where the integrand is in the form of 1/(a+bcosx),1/(a+bsinx) or 1/(a+bcosx+csinx)

Type I 

Integrals of the form:

$\int  \frac{dx}{a+b\text{cos}x}  \text{ or }  \int  \frac{dx}{a+b\text{sin}x} \text{ or }  \int  \frac{dx}{a+b\text{cos}x+c\text{sin}x}$

Working Rule: 

Put $\text{sin}x=\frac{2\text{tan}\frac{x}{2}}{1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\frac{x}{2}}$ and $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ cos}x=\frac{1-\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\frac{x}{2}}{1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\frac{x}{2}}$

whichever is needed and then put $z=\text{tan}\frac{x}{2}$

Q) Evaluate $\int \frac{dx}{4+5\text{sin}x}$ 

Solution : $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int \frac{dx}{4+5\text{sin}x}=\int \frac{dx}{4+5\cdot \frac{2\text{tan}\frac{x}{2}}{1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\frac{x}{2}}}$ 

$~=\int ~\frac{1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\frac{x}{2}}{4\left( 1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\frac{x}{2} \right)+10\text{tan}\frac{x}{2}}dx$

$~=\int ~\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\frac{x}{2}}{4\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\frac{x}{2}+10\text{tan}\frac{x}{2}+4}dx$ … (i)

Let $\text{z}=\text{tan}\frac{\text{x}}{2}$, then $\text{dz}=\frac{1}{2}\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\frac{\text{x}}{2}\text{dx}$

From (i), $I=\int \frac{2dz}{4{{z}^{2}}+10z+4}=\int \frac{dz}{2{{z}^{2}}+5z+2}$

$~=\int ~\frac{\text{d}z}{2\left( {{\text{z}}^{2}}+\frac{5}{2}\text{z}+1 \right)}=\frac{1}{2}\int ~\frac{\text{dz}}{{{\text{z}}^{2}}+2\text{z}\cdot \frac{5}{4}+\frac{25}{16}-\frac{25}{16}+1}$

$~=\frac{1}{2}\int ~\frac{\text{dz}}{{{\left( \text{z}+\frac{5}{4} \right)}^{2}}-{{\left( \frac{3}{4} \right)}^{2}}}$ … (ii)

Let $\text{y}=\text{z}+\frac{5}{4}$, then $\text{dy}=\text{dz}$

From (ii), $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\frac{1}{2}\int \frac{\text{dy}}{{{\text{y}}^{2}}-{{\text{a}}^{2}}}$ where $\text{a}=\frac{3}{4}$

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}} {} & ~=\frac{1}{2}\cdot \frac{1}{2a}\text{log}\left| \frac{\text{y}-\text{a}}{\text{y}+\text{a}} \right|+\text{c}=\frac{1}{2}\cdot \frac{1}{2\cdot \frac{3}{4}\text{log}}\left| \frac{\text{z}+\frac{5}{4}-\frac{3}{4}}{\text{z}+\frac{5}{4}+\frac{3}{4}} \right|+\text{c} \\ {} & ~=\frac{1}{3}\text{log}\left| \frac{2\text{z}+1}{2\left( \text{z}+2 \right)} \right|+\text{c}=\frac{1}{3}\text{log}\left| \frac{2\text{tan}\frac{\text{x}}{2}+1}{2\left( \text{tan}\frac{\text{x}}{2}+2 \right)} \right|+\text{c} \\ \end{array}$

Q) Evaluate $\int \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}\text{sin}x+\text{cos}x}dx$ 

Solution: 

Let $\sqrt{3}=r\text{sin}\theta $ and $1=r\text{cos}\theta $. Then

$\begin{array}{*{35}{r}}  {} & r=\sqrt{{{(3)}^{2}}+{{1}^{2}}}=2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ and }\!\!~\!\!\text{ tan}\theta =\frac{\sqrt{3}}{1}\Rightarrow \theta =\frac{\pi }{3} \\ \therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ } & ~\int ~\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}\text{sin}x+\text{cos}x}dx=\int ~\frac{1}{r\text{sin}\theta \text{sin}x+r\text{cos}\theta \text{cos}x}dx \\ \end{array}$

$~=\frac{1}{\text{r}}\int ~\frac{1}{\text{cos}\left( \text{x}-\theta \right)}\text{dx}=\frac{1}{\text{r}}\int ~\text{sec}\left( \text{x}-\theta \right)\text{dx}=\frac{1}{\text{r}}\text{log}\left| \text{tan}\left( \frac{\pi }{4}+\frac{\text{x}}{2}-\frac{\theta }{2} \right) \right|+\text{C}$

$~=\frac{1}{2}\text{log}\left| \text{tan}\left( \frac{\pi }{4}+\frac{\text{x}}{2}-\frac{\pi }{6} \right) \right|+\text{C}=\frac{1}{2}\text{log}\left| \text{tan}\left( \frac{\text{x}}{2}+\frac{\pi }{12} \right) \right|+\text{C}$

concepts.title: Solving Trigonometric Integrals where the integrand is in the form of  sinx/(asinx+bcosx),cosx/(asinx+bcosx) or (pcosx+qsinx)/(bcosx+csinx)
concepts.conceptBody:
Solving Trigonometric Integrals where the integrand is in the form of sinx/(asinx+bcosx),cosx/(asinx+bcosx) or (pcosx+qsinx)/(bcosx+csinx)

Integrals of the form $\int rac{ ext{sin}x}{a ext{sin}x+b ext{cos}x}dx,\int rac{ ext{cos}x}{a ext{sin}x+b ext{cos}x}dx$, or $\int rac{p ext{sin}x+q ext{cos}x}{c ext{sin}x+b ext{cos}x}dx$ 

where $a e 0,b e 0$

Working Rule:

Step $-1:$ Put Numerator $=A$ (denominator) $+B$ (derivative of denominator. $)$ where$a e 0,b e 0$

Step -2 : Then equate the coefficients of $ ext{sin}x$ and $ ext{cosx}$ to find A and B.

Q) (i) $\int rac{3 ext{sin}x+2 ext{cos}x}{3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x}dx$ 

(ii) $\int rac{dx}{3+4 ext{cot}x}$ 

Solution: 

(i) $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }I=\int rac{3 ext{sin}x+2 ext{cos}x}{3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x}dx$

Let $3 ext{sin}x+2 ext{cos}x=A\left( 3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x ight)+B\left\{ rac{d}{dx}[3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x\} ight.$

$\Rightarrow ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }3 ext{sinx}+2 ext{cosx}= ext{A}\left( 3 ext{cosx}+2 ext{sinx} ight)+ ext{B}\left( -3 ext{sinx}+2 ext{cosx} ight)$

Comparing the coefficient of $ ext{sinx}$ and $ ext{cosx}$, we get

$3=2 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ A}-3 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ B}$ and $2=3 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ A}+2 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ B}$

Solving them, we get

$ ext{A}= rac{12}{13}$ and $ ext{B}=- rac{5}{13}$

$ egin{array}{*{35}{r}} herefore ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }I & ~=\int ~ rac{ rac{12}{13}\left( 3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x ight)- rac{5}{13}\left( -3 ext{sin}x+2 ext{cos}x ight)}{3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x}dx \ {} & ~= rac{12}{13}\int ~ rac{3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x}{3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x}dx- rac{5}{13}\int ~ rac{-3 ext{sin}x+2 ext{cos}x}{3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x}dx \ \end{array}$

$= rac{12}{13}\int ~1 ext{dx}- rac{5}{13}\int ~ rac{ ext{dt}}{ ext{t}}$

where $t=3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x$

$\Rightarrow ext{dt}=\left( -3 ext{sinx}+2 ext{cosx} ight) ext{dx}$

$= rac{12}{13}x- rac{5}{13} ext{log}\left| t ight|+c= rac{12}{13}x- rac{5}{13} ext{log}\left| 3 ext{cos}x+2 ext{sin}x ight|+c$

(ii) $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }I=\int rac{dx}{3+4 ext{cot}x}=\int rac{dx}{3+4 rac{ ext{cos}x}{ ext{sin}x}}=\int rac{ ext{sin}xdx}{3 ext{sin}x+4 ext{cos}x}$

Let $ ext{sin}x=A\left( 3 ext{sin}x+4 ext{cos}x ight)+B\left( rac{d}{dx}\left( 3 ext{sin}x+4 ext{cos}x ight) ight.$

$\Rightarrow ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ sinx}= ext{A}\left( 3 ext{sinx}+4 ext{cosx} ight)+ ext{B}\left( 3 ext{cosx}-4 ext{sinx} ight)$

Comparing the coefficients of $ ext{sinx}$ and $ ext{cosx}$, we get

$1=3 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ A}-4 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ B}$ and $0=4 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ A}+3 ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ B}$

Solving them, we get

$ ext{A}= rac{3}{25}, ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ B}=- rac{4}{25}$

$ herefore $I $~=\int ~ rac{3}{25} rac{\left( 3 ext{sin}x+4 ext{cos}x ight)- rac{4}{25}\left( 3 ext{cos}x-4 ext{sin}x ight)}{3 ext{sin}x+4 ext{cos}x}$
