$~= rac{3}{25}\int ~ rac{3 ext{sin}x+4 ext{cos}x}{3 ext{sin}x+4 ext{cos}x}dx- rac{4}{25}\int ~ rac{3 ext{cos}x-4 ext{sin}x}{3 ext{sin}x+4 ext{cos}x}dx$

$~= rac{3}{25}\int ~1dx- rac{4}{25}\int ~ rac{dt}{t}$

where $t=3 ext{sin}x+4 ext{cos}x$

$\Rightarrow $ $dt$ $~=\left( -3 ext{cos}x+4 ext{sin}x ight)dx$

I $~= rac{3}{25}x- rac{4}{25} ext{log}\left| t ight|+c= rac{3}{25}x- rac{4}{25} ext{log}\left| 3 ext{sin}x+4 ext{cos}x ight|+c$

Q) Evaluate $\int rac{ ext{sin}x}{ ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x}dx$ [I.I.T. 78] 

Solution: 

Let $ ext{sinx}= ext{A}\left( ext{sinx}- ext{cosx} ight)+ ext{B}$ d.c. of $\left( ext{sinx}- ext{cosx} ight)$

or, $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ sinx}= ext{A}\left( ext{sinx}- ext{cosx} ight)+ ext{B}\left( ext{cosx}+ ext{sinx} ight)$

or, $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ sinx}=\left( ext{A}+ ext{B} ight) ext{sinx}+\left( ext{B}- ext{A} ight) ext{cosx}$ … (i)

Equating the coefficients of $ ext{sin}x$ and $ ext{cos}x$, we get

$ ext{A}+ ext{B}=1$ (i) and $ ext{B}- ext{A}=0$...(ii)

Solving (i) and (ii) we get $ ext{A}= rac{1}{2}, ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ B}= rac{1}{2}$

From (i), $ ext{sin}x= rac{1}{2}\left( ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x ight)+ rac{1}{2}\left( ext{cos}x+ ext{sin}x ight)$

Now $ ext{ }\!\!~\!\! ext{ }I=\int rac{ ext{sin}x}{ ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x}dx$

$~=\int ~ rac{ rac{1}{2}\left( ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x ight)+ rac{1}{2}\left( ext{cos}x+ ext{sin}x ight)}{ ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x}dx$

$~= rac{1}{2}\int ~ rac{ ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x}{ ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x}dx+ rac{1}{2}\int ~ rac{ ext{cos}x+ ext{sin}x}{ ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x}dx$

$~= rac{1}{2}\int ~dx+ rac{1}{2}\int ~ rac{ ext{cos}x+ ext{sin}x}{ ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x}dx$

$~= rac{x}{2}+ rac{1}{2} ext{log}\left| ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x ight|+c$

[Putting, $z= ext{sin}x- ext{cos}x$, so that $dz=\left( ext{cos}x+ ext{sin}x ight)dx]$

concepts.title: Solving Trignometric Integrals where the integrand is in the form of (asinx+bcosx+c)/(psinx+qcosx+r)
concepts.conceptBody:
Solving Trignometric Integrals where the integrand is in the form of (asinx+bcosx+c)/(psinx+qcosx+r)

Working Rule: 

To evaluate this type of integrals, we use the following algorithm

(i) Write Numerator $=\lambda $ (Diff. of denominator) $+\mu $ (Denominator) $+v$

i.e. $a\text{sin}x+b\text{cos}x+c=\lambda \left( p\text{cos}x-q\text{sin}x \right)+\mu \left( p\text{sin}x+q\text{cos}x+r \right)+v$

(ii) Obtain the values of $\lambda $ and $\mu $ by equating the coefficient of sinx and cosx and the constant terms on both the sides

(iii) Replace numerator in the integrand by $\lambda \left( p\text{cos}x-q\text{sin}x \right)+\mu \left( p\text{sin}x+q\text{cos}x+r \right)+v$ to obtain

$~\int ~\frac{a\text{sin}x+b\text{cos}x+c}{p\text{sin}x+q\text{cos}x+r}dx$

$~=\lambda \int ~\frac{p\text{cos}x-q\text{sin}x}{p\text{sin}x+q\text{cos}x+r}dx+\mu \int ~\frac{p\text{sin}x+q\text{cos}x+r}{p\text{sin}x+q\text{cos}x+r}+v\int ~\frac{1}{p\text{sin}x+q\text{cos}x+r}dx$

$=\lambda \text{log}\left| p\text{sin}x+q\text{cos}x+r \right|+\mu x+v\int ~\frac{1}{p\text{sin}x+q\text{cos}x+r}dx$

(iv) Evaluate the integral on RHS in step III by using the method discussed earlier.

Q) Evaluate $\int \frac{3\text{cos}x+2}{\text{sin}x+2\text{cos}x+3}dx$ 

Solution: 

$I=\int \frac{3\text{cos}x+2}{\text{sin}x+2\text{cos}x+3}dx$

Let $3\text{cosx}+2=\lambda \left( \text{sinx}+2\text{cosx}+3 \right)+\mu \left( \text{cosx}-2\text{sinx} \right)+\text{v}$

Comparing the coefficients of sinx, $\text{cos}x$ and constant term on both sides, we get

$\lambda -2\mu =0,2\lambda +\mu =3,3\lambda +v=2$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }\lambda =\frac{6}{5},\mu =\frac{3}{5}$ and $\text{v}=-\frac{8}{5}$

$\therefore \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int \frac{\lambda \left( \text{sin}x+2\text{cos}x+3 \right)+\mu \left( \text{cos}x-2\text{sin}x \right)+v}{\text{sin}x+2\text{cos}x+3}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\lambda \int dx+\mu \int \frac{\text{cos}x-2\text{sin}x}{\text{sin}x+2\text{cos}x+3}dx+v\int \frac{1}{\text{sin}x+2\text{cos}x+3}dx$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\lambda x+\mu \text{log}\left| \text{sin}x+2\text{cos}x+3 \right|+\text{v}{{\text{I}}_{1}}$, where

${{I}_{1}}=\int \frac{1}{\text{sin}x+2\text{cos}x+3}dx$

Putting $\text{sin}x=\frac{2\text{tan}x/2}{1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x/2},\text{cos}x=\frac{1-\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x/2}{1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x/2}$, we get

${{I}_{1}}=\int \frac{1}{\frac{2\text{tan}x/2}{1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x/2}+\frac{2\left( 1-\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x/2 \right)}{1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x/2}+3}dx$

$=\int \frac{1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x/2}{2\text{tan}x/2+2-2\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x/2+3\left( 1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x/2 \right)}dx=\int \frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x/2}{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x/2+2\text{tan}x/2+5}dx$

Putting $\text{tan}\frac{\text{x}}{2}=\text{t}$ and $\frac{1}{2}\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\frac{\text{x}}{2}\text{dx}=\text{dt}$, or $\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\frac{\text{x}}{2}\text{dx}=2\text{dt}$, we get

${{I}_{1}}=\int ~\frac{2dt}{{{t}^{2}}+2t+5}=2\int ~\frac{dt}{{{(t+1)}^{2}}+{{2}^{2}}}=\frac{2}{2}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{t+1}{2} \right)=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{\text{tan}\frac{x}{2}+1}{2} \right)$

Hence, $I=\lambda x+\mu \text{log}\left| \text{sin}x+2\text{cos}x+3 \right|+v\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{\text{tan}\frac{x}{2}+1}{2} \right)+C$

Where $\lambda =\frac{6}{5},\mu =\frac{3}{5}$ and  $v=-\frac{8}{5}$

concepts.title: Solving Trignometric Integrals where the integrand is in the form of 1/[a+b(cosx)^2],1/[a+b(sinx)^2],1/[a+b(sinx)^2+c(cosx)^2],1/[a(sinx)^2+b(cosx)^2],1/[(asinx+bcosx)^2]
concepts.conceptBody:
Solving Trignometric Integrals where the integrand is in the form of $1/[a+b(cosx)^2],1/[a+b(sinx)^2],1/[a+b(sinx)^2+c(cosx)^2],1/[a(sinx)^2+b(cosx)^2],1/[(asinx+bcosx)^2]$

$\int ~\frac{1}{a\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+b\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x}dx\cdot \int ~\frac{1}{a+b\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}dx,\int ~\frac{1}{a+b\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x}dx,\int ~\frac{1}{{{(a\text{sin}x+b\text{cos}x)}^{2}}}dx,\int ~\frac{1}{a+b\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x}dx$

Working Rule:

To evaluate this type of integrals we use the following algorithm:

(i) Divide numerator and denominator both by $\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\text{x}$

(ii) Replace $\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x$, if any, in denominator by $1+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x$

(iii) Put $\text{tanx}=\text{t}$ so that $\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}\text{x}d\text{x}=\text{dt}$

This substitution reduces the integral in the form $\int \frac{1}{a{{t}^{2}}+bt+c}dt$

(iv) Evaluate the integral obtained in step III by using the methods discussed earlier.

Q) (i) $\int \frac{dx}{3\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+8\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x+1}$ 
(iv) $\int \frac{dx}{3-2\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}$ 

Solution: 

(i) $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }I=\int \frac{dx}{3\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+8\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}x+1}$

Dividing numerator and denominator by $\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\text{x}$, we get

$I=\int ~\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx}{3\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+8+\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x}=\int ~\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx}{3\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+8+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+1}$

$=\int ~\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx}{4\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+9}$

Putting $\text{tan}x=t\Rightarrow \text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx=dt$

I $~=\int ~\frac{dt}{4{{t}^{2}}+9}=\frac{1}{4}\int ~\frac{dt}{{{t}^{2}}+\frac{9}{4}}=\frac{1}{4}\int ~\frac{dt}{{{t}^{2}}+{{\left( \frac{3}{2} \right)}^{2}}}$

$~=\frac{1}{4}\frac{1}{\frac{3}{2}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{\text{t}}{\frac{3}{2}}+c=\frac{1}{6}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{2\text{t}}{3}+\text{c}$

$~=\frac{1}{6}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{2\text{tanx}}{3} \right)+\text{c}$

(iv) $I=\int \frac{dx}{3-2\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}$

Dividing numerator and denominator by $\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{2}}\text{x}$, we get

I $~=\int ~\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx}{3\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}x-2\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}=\int ~\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx}{3\left( \text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+1 \right)-2\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}dx$

$~=\int ~\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx}{3\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+3-2\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x}dx=\int ~\frac{\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{2}}xdx}{\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{2}}x+3}$

$~=\int ~\frac{dt}{{{t}^{2}}+{{(\sqrt{3})}^{2}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\frac{t}{\sqrt{3}}+c=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{\text{tan}x}{\sqrt{3}} \right)+c$
